ONLINE 题干解析： 表格为两家公司董事薪酬。 Cadnam Co 20X6预测 Cadnam Co 财务总监编制的20X6 预测如下： - Cadnam Co20X6年的税前营业利润将为$2,678m, 相比20X5 年增长了3%。营业利 润率为2%, 与 2 0X5 相同。 - 税率为30%。 - 20X6 年的平均债务为$10,250m, 预计年底负债率为41.3%。平均税前债务利率为8%。 - 在20X6年，为将非流动资产保持在当前产能所需的投资为$2.43m, 已作为折旧计 入营业利润计算。 20X6 年，额外的资产投资为：每增加1美元收入，需要0.25美元额外资产投资。 (a)Calculate the forecast dividend capacity of Cadnam Co for 20X6. (5 marks) Requirements解析： 计算 Cadnam 公司20X6年的股利能力。 Marking Scheme: (a) Interest Tax Investment in additional assets Depreciation 答案： CATION(a)Dividend capacity CATION Operating profit Less:Interest(8%×$10,250m) Less:Taxation(30%x($2,678m-$820m)) assets(25%x0·03x$2,678m/(1·03×0·02))Less:Investment in additional Forecast dividend capacity assets(25%x0·03x$2,678m/(1·03×0·02)) $m 2,678 (820) (557) (975) 326 答案解析： 以下为教材中给出的计算 dividend capacity的过程，股权自由现金流是企业经营现金流 在满足生产经营所需后可供分配给股东的部分。 Sales Cost of sales Contribution(Gross profit) Other expenses(include depreciation) Operating profit(PBIT) Tax +Depreciation Operating cashflow Investmentin non-current asset Additional NCA investment Replacement&Incremental NCA investment Investment in incremental working capital Free Cash Flows to Firm(FCFF) -Net effect of interest(interest aftertax) -Repayment of debt +Issue of debts Free Cash Flows to Equity(FCFE) /Dividend Capacity 经营利润为$2,678m, 从原则上要加上折旧$2,430m, 但是这里 incremental NCA investment也是$2,430m, 所以就没有必要先加上$2,430m 再减去$2,430m了 。 所以在本题中，直接减去所得税，Investment required in additional assets,和给到债权 人的利息即可。 · 所得税：经营利润为$2,678m, 利息为8%(平均利率) x$10,250m (平均债务)= $820m, 所得税率为20%,所以 Taxation=30%×($2,678m-$820m)=$557m。 · Investment required in additional assets:题中表明收入增加$1,Investment required in additional assets 增加$0 ·25。20X6 年 pre-tax operating profit 为2,678m,OPM 为2%,所以20x6年 Revenue=2,678m/2%, 而20X6 年 pre-tax operating profit 为 20×5年的1.03 倍且20×5 年的 OPM 同 样 为 2 % , 所 以 2 0x5 年的 Revenue= (2,678m/1.03)/2%, 由此可得，20×6的revenue 比20×5的 revenue 多2,678m/2%- (2,678m/1.03)/2%, 即003×2,678m/(1-03×0·02)。所以investment in additional assets =25%×0·03×$2,678m/(103×0·02)=975m · 给到债权人的利息 Interest=8%×10,250m=2,678m。 综上，Forecast dividend capacity=2,678-820-557-975=326m。 (b)Discuss the viability and financial impacts of Cadnam Co seeking to maintain its current dividend policy, supporting your answers with relevant calculations. Note:6 marks are available for calculations in part(b). (12 marks) Requirements解析： 讨论Cadnam Co维持当前股息政策的可行性和财务影响，并用相关计算支持您的答案。 Marking Scheme: (b)Calculations Growth in PAT 1-2 Dividend payout ratios 1-2 Growth in dividends 1-2 Residual profit 1-2 Growth in share price 1-2 Calculations Max 6 Discussion Dividends Gearing Share price 答 案 ： (b)Growth in profit after tax Cadnam Holmsley Dividend payout ratios 20X3 % 8 ·0 7 ·1 20X4 % 4 ·0 6 ·9 20X5 % 1 ·9 7 ·6 Geometric mean annual growth rate % 4 ·6 7 ·2 Cadnam Holmsley Growth in dividends 20X2 % 55 ·4 37 ·7 20X3 % 56 ·4 37 ·1 20X4 % 59 ·7 36 ·5 20X5 % 64 ·6 35 ·7 20X3 % 20X4 % 20X5 % Geometric mean annual growth rate % Cadnam 9 ·8 10 ·1 10 ·3 10 ·1 Holmsley 5 ·4 5 ·3 5 ·3 5 ·3 Residual profit(after tax-profit for the year-dividend-new investment) 20X2 $m 20X3 $m 20X4 $m 20X5 $m Cadnam 333 338 41 (304) Holmsley 330 375 419 486 Growth in share price 20X3 20X4 20X5 Geometric mean annual growth rate % % % % Cadnam 9 ·6 4 ·9 2 ·5 5 ·6 Holmsley 3 ·8 6 ·1 6 ·8 5 ·6 Comments Dividends Both companies have shown fairly consistent increases over the last three years,with Cadnam Co's dividends increasing at around 10%each year,and Holmsley Co's dividends increasing at around 5%over the last three years.However,Holmsley Co's policy appears to be sustainable at present,whereas it is doubtful whether Cadnam Co's policy is sustainable. Holmsley Co has managed to increase dividends,gradually also increasing investment in aditional assets and residual profits, whilst at the same time having a decreasing dividend pay-out ratio. In order to maintain its rate of dividend increase,however,Cadnam Co has had to pay out an increasing proportion of earnings each year.It seems that Cadnam Co may have sustained dividend increases up to 20X3 at the expense of additional investment, and is now having to increase additional investment significantly in order to make up for previous under-investment.In 20×5, Cadnam Co's residual profits became negative,and the dividend capacity calculation for 20X6 suggests that a much lower level of dividends would be appropriate. Gearing Holmsley Co's gearing appears to be stable at around the average for the industry,suggesting that perhaps it has found its optimum level.Up to 20X4,Cadnam Co could perhaps have been taking advantage of debt capacity to increase its debt towards this level.However,its gearing now appears to be on a rising trend,of necessity increasing significantly in 20X5 to fund both additional investment and increasing dividends,despite the rise in its share price.Cadnam Co's gearing is predicted to increase further in 20X6,but how long this is sustainable is uncertain. Share price The average increase for both companies over the last four years has been the same.However,the percentage increases over the last two years for Holmsley Co has been higher than for Cadnam Co.This suggests that the market has placed more significance on the higher %growth in Holmsley Co's after-tax profits than in Cadnam Co's higher %growth in dividends, maybe seeing this as an indication that Holmsley Co's strategy has been more successful and is more likely in future to deliver higher share price growth. 答案解析： ● 首先计算相关指标 √Growth in profit after tax:如 2 0x3 年 Cadnam 利润增长率=(1,490-1,380)/1,380=8%。 √ Dividend payout ratios 股利支付率= Dividend/Profit after tax: 如20x3 年 Cadnam 的股利支付率=765/1,380=55 ·4%。 √ Growth in dividends: 如20x3 年 Cadnam 股利增长率=(840-765)/765=9.8%。 √ Residual profit 利润留存，根据答案中所写公式计算即可。 √ Growth in share price:如20x3年 Cadnam 股价增长幅度为(5.35-4.88)/4.88=9.6%。 ● 评价 ● 股息 在过去三年中，两家公司都表现出了相当一致的增长，Cadnam Co的股息增长率约为每 年 10%, Holmsley 公司约为5%。但是，HolmsleyCo 的政策目前看来是可持续的，而 Cadnam Co 的政策是否可持续值得怀疑。 Holmsley 公司设法增加了股息，并逐步增加了对额外资产投资和剩余利润，同时股息支 付率在下降。 然而，Cadnam Co 为了保持其股息增长率，不得不分配越来越高比例的收益。Cadnam Co 似乎以牺牲额外投资为代价，将股息持续增加至20X3,现在必须大幅增加额外投资， 以弥补之前的投资不足。在20X5中 ，Cadnam Co 的剩余利润为负值，20X6的股息能 力计算表明支付低水平的股息将是适当的。 ● 杠杆率 Holmsley Co的杠杆率似乎稳定在行业平均水平左右，这表明它可能已经找到了最佳水 平。到20X4 时 ，Cadnam Co可能会朝着这个水平增加债务。然而，其杠杆率现在似乎 呈上升趋势，在20X5 大幅上涨，为额外投资和增加股息募集资金，尽管其股价依然在 上涨。预计 Cadnam Co的杠杆率20X6 将进一步增加，但这可持续多久还不确定。 ● 股价 过去四年中，两家公司的平均增长率相同。然而，在过去的两年中，Holmsley 公司的股 价增长率一直高于 Cadnam 公司。这表明市场已经更加重视 Holmsley 公司的高税后利 润增长率，相比于 Cadnam 公司的高股息增长率。也许这表明Holmsley 公司的战略更 加成功，未来更有可能实现更高的股价增长。 (c)Discuss the governance and ethical issues associated with Cadnam Co's dividend and directors'remuneration policies. (8 marks) Requirements解析： 结合股息和董事薪酬政策，讨论 Cadnam Co的公司治理和道德问题。 Marking Scheme (c) Dividend policy statement Up to 5 Directors'remuneration Up to 5 Max 8 答案： (c)Dividend policy One possible question is whether the statement in the annual report fairly reflects the likely future dividend policy of Cadnam Co.The report gives the impression that the current dividend policy will be sustained,whereas the figures suggest that this may not be the case.If the policy proves not to be sustainable,it would suggest a failure either of integrity(if the directors made a statement with a high risk that it would not be true)or due care(that they failed to take into account indicators which suggested their policy is not sustainable).The directors may be questioned by the auditors about whether this statement is true and fair. There is also the question of balancing the interests of different stakeholders.To some degree,criticism of rises in dividend and director remuneration levels versus increases in employee salary levels could be said to be a matter of opinion. However,the fact that there is a government enquiry into low pay in the sector suggests that pay levels are lower than society deems desirable.The force of the criticisms may be enhanced by the statements which Cadnam Co has made about developing an integrated reporting approach.Integrated reporting is not just about extra details in the annual report,but also reflects an underlying policy of carrying out business which includes responsiveness to the needs of different stakeholders. The dividend capacity and gearing figures may also call into question whether Cadnam Co's board is taking excessive risks. Does paying out increasing dividends in future mean that the company is likely to have inadequate resources to sustain its business,and may be jeopardising the interests of lenders and employees as well as stakeholders?Certainly there appears to be doubts about maintenance of future income levels with a number of contracts coming up for renewal and terms possibly being tightened by clients.Clients may be doubtful about renewing contracts if Cadnam Co's solvency appears doubtful. Directors'remuneration The directors'remuneration packages also raise concerns.Comparison with Holmsley Co shows that salary,which is not dependent on performance,is a more significant element of the remuneration packages at Cadnam Co than Holmsley Co.Both companies have bonuses which depend to some degree on performance.However,Cadnam Co's directors are also rewarded by loyalty bonuses,which again do not depend on performance but staying with the company.Holmsley Co has a share option scheme in place,which would seem to reward longer-term good performance,although it cannot be determined how significant a part of remuneration share options willbe.Cadnam Co's remuneration scheme appears only to reward short-term profitability, possibly meaning that the directors may neglect the longer-term success and possibly even viability of the company. 答案解析： ● 股利政策 一个可能的问题是，年报中的声明是否公平地反映了Cadnam Co 未来可能的股息政策 该报告给人的印象是，目前的股息政策将持续下去，而数字表明，这可能会事实并非如 此。如果该政策被证明是不可持续的，这将意味着诚信的失败(如果董事做出了具有很 高风险的声明，即这是不真实的)或应有的注意(他们没有考虑到建议的指标他们的政 策是不可持续的)。审计员可能会询问董事们该陈述是否真实和公正。 还有一个平衡不同利益相关者利益的问题。在某种程度上，对股息和董事薪酬水平与员 工工资水平的增加可以说是见仁见智的问题。 然而，政府对该行业的低工资进行了调查，这一事实表明，工资水平低于社会水平认为 可取。Cadnam Co关于开发综合报告方法。综合报告不仅涉及年度报告中的额外细节， 还反映了开展业务的基本政策，包括对不同利益相关者需求的响应。 股息能力和杠杆率数据也可能会让人质疑Cadnam Co董事会是否承担了过高的风险。 未来支付越来越多的股息是否意味着公司可能没有足够的资源来维持业务，并可能危及 贷款人和员工以及利益相关者的利益?当然，似乎对未来收入水平的维持存在疑问，可 能会有一些合同需要续签和条款被客户收紧。如果Cadnam Co的偿付能力出现疑问， 客户可能会怀疑是否续签合同。 ● 董事薪酬 董事的薪酬待遇也令人担忧。与Holmsley Co的比较表明与Holmsley 公司相比，Cadnam 公司的薪酬待遇更重要的一个因素是绩效公司的奖金在一定程度上取决于业绩。然而， Cadnam Co的董事也会得到奖励。 通过忠诚度奖金，这同样不取决于业绩，而是取决于公司的忠诚度。Holmsley 公司拥有 股票期权该计划似乎会奖励长期良好的业绩，尽管尚无法确定其重要性 Cadnam Co的 薪酬计划似乎只是为了奖励短期盈利能力，这可能意味着董事们可能会忽视公司的长期 成功，甚至可能忽视公司的生存能力。 3 A new client has approached you for advice on a potential acquisition.Kerrin Co is a consumer electronics manufacturer and retailer.The company obtained a listing eight years ago with the founders retaining a 20%stake in the business. Whilst Kerrin Co had previously experienced rapid growth in earnings before tax,problems arose soon after the listing as competition intensified.Although the company remains profitable,annual growth has declined significantly and is currently 3%. The board is concerned by the lack of future growth opportunities.The current share price reflects these concerns, trading well below the offer price of eight years ago.In response,the directors have decided to invest in a market development strategy for future growth,utilising significant cash reserves to acquire companies in other areas of the country where competition is less intense.The board has identified a potential target,Danton Co. Danton Co Danton Co is a privately owned consumer electronics company,established ten years ago.Significant unrelieved losses were incurred in the early years of development although the company is now profitable and achieving growth in earnings before tax of 6%per year.However,cash reserves are low.Access to capital has acted as a severe constraint on Danton Co's reinvestment potential throughout this period.The founders and their families own 60%of the shares with the balance held by a venture capitalist organisation,which acquired its equity stake around six years ago. Acquisition information Kerrin Co's board is keen to ensure that Danton Co's founders remain as directors after the acquisition and the company has sufficient cash reserves to purchase Danton Co outright. Early discussions between the directors of both companies suggest Danton Co's shareholders would approve a cash offer of $13·10 per share.As an alternative,the board is considering a share-for-share exchange to fund the acquisition in order to preserve cash for future acquisitions and dividend payments.Recent mergers have attracted an acquisition premium of around 25%-30%and Danton Co's directors indicated their shareholders would be expecting a premium towards the higher end of this scale for a share-for-share offer.Kerrin Co has therefore asked you to design a share-for-share offer scheme which willallow for a 30%acquisition premium.You have been provided with extracts from the latest financial statements for both companies. Extracts from the most recent financial statements Kerrin Co Danton Co $m $m Operating profit 448·6 201·8 Earnings before tax 381·9 116·3 Additional financial information The book value of Kerrin Co's $0·50 ordinary shares is $375m.These shares are currently trading at $5·28 and the finance director expects the price earnings(PE)ratio to increase by 10%if the acquisition proceeds. Danton Co upgraded its main manufacturing facility during the previous year and expects to make annual pre-tax cost savings of $2·5m from the start of the current financial year.The book value of Danton Co's $0·25 ordinary shares is $35m.Based on an analysis of companies of a comparable size and cost structure,it is estimated that Danton Co's PE ratio is 20%higher than Kerrin Co's current PE ratio. Kerrin Co's chief executive officer estimates annual pre-tax revenue and cost synergies of $15·2m to arise as a result of the acquisition.In addition,the finance director anticipates annual pre-tax financial synergies of $5·3m although she insists this is a cautious estimate after reading an article on recent merger and acquisition activity where post-acquisition synergies have either been overestimated or failed to materialise. The rate of corporation tax relevant to both companies is 20%. Required: (a)Discuss possible sources of financial synergy arising from Kerrin Co's acquisition of Danton Co and comment on the finance director's concern that synergy is often overestimated,including any steps which could be taken by Kerrin Co's board to address this problem. (8 marks) (b) Advise the directors on a suitable share-for-share exchange offer which meets the criteria specified by Danton Co's shareholders and calculate the effect of the cash and share-for-share offers on the post-acquisition wealth of both Kerrin Co's and Danton Co's shareholders. (13 marks) (c)Discuss the likely reaction of Kerrin Co's and Danton Co's shareholders to the cash and share-for-share offers. (4 marks) A new client has approached you for advice on a potential acquisition.Kerrin Co is a consumer electronics manufacturer and retailer.The company obtained a listing eight years ago with the founders retaining a 20%stake in the business. Whilst Kerrin Co had previously experienced rapid growth in earnings before tax,problems arose soon after the listing as competition intensified.Although the company remains profitable,annual growth has declined significantly and is currently 3%. The board is concerned by the lack of future growth opportunities.The current share price reflects these concerns, trading well below the offer price of eight years ago.In response,the directors have decided to invest in a market development strategy for future growth,utilising significant cash reserves to acquire companies in other areas of the country where competition is less intense.The board has identified a potential target,Danton Co. 题干解析： 一位新客户向您寻求收购建议。 Kerrin Co是一家消费电子产品制造商和零售商。该公司 八年前上市，创始人保留了该公司20%的股份。 尽管 Kerrin Co 此前经历了税前收益的快速增长，但是随着竞争加剧，公司上市后不久 就出现了问题。尽管该公司保持盈利，但年增长率大幅下降目前为3%。 董事会担心缺乏未来的增长机会。当前股价反映了这些担忧，交易价格远低于八年前的 报价。作为回应，董事们为未发发展指定市场拓展战略，利用大量现金储备去收购其他 竞争不那么激烈的国家的公司。董事会已经确定了一个潜在的目标，Danton Co。 Danton Co Danton Co is a privately owned consumer electronics company,established ten years ago.Significant unrelieved losses were incurred in the early years of development although the company is now profitable and achieving growth in earnings before tax of 6%per year.However,cash reserves are low.Access to capital has acted as a severe constraint on Danton Co's reinvestment potential throughout this period.The founders and their families own 60%of the shares with the balance held by a venture capitalist organisation,which acquired its equity stake around six years ago. 题干解析： Danton Co 是一家私营消费电子公司，成立于十年前。尽管该公司现在盈利，并在税前 收益每年6%,但是在发展早期存在重大未弥补损失。然而，现金储备很低。获得资本渠 道是 Danton Co 公司在此期间的再投资潜力的一个严重的制约因素。创始人及其家族拥 有60%的股份，剩余部分由一家风险投资机构持有，该机构在大约六年前收购了其股权。 Acquisition information Kerrin Co's board is keen to ensure that Danton Co's founders remain as directors after the acquisition and the company has sufficient cash reserves to purchase Danton Co outright. Early discussions between the directors of both companies suggest Danton Co's shareholders would approve a cash offer of $13·10 per share.As an alternative,the board is considering a share-for-share exchange to fund the acquisition in order to preserve cash for future acquisitions and dividend payments.Recent mergers have attracted an acquisition premium of around 25%-30%and Danton Co's directors indicated their shareholders would be expecting a premium towards the higher end of this scale for a share-for-share offer.Kerrin Co has therefore asked you to design a share-for-share offer scheme which willallow for a 30%acquisition premium.You have been provided with extracts from the latest financial statements for both companies. 题干解析： Kerrin Co 董事希望确保Danton Co的创始人在收购和公司成立后继续担任董事，并且 公司有足够的现金储备直接购买Danton Co。 两家公司董事之间的早期讨论表明， Danton Co的股东将批准每股13.10美元的现金报 价。作为替代方案，董事会正在考虑进行股份交换来完成收购，以便为未来的收购和股 息支付保留现金。最近的合并产生的收购溢价约为25%-30%,并且 Danton Co的董事表 示，他们的股东希望通过以股换股来获得的比较最高的溢价(即30%)。因此， Kerrin Co 要求您设计允许30%收购溢价的以股换股要约计划。已向您提供摘录这两家公司的最新 财务报表。 Extracts from the most recent financial statements Kerrin Co Danton Co $m $m Earnings before tax 381 9 116 3Operating profit 448 ··6 201 ··8 Earnings before tax 381 9 116 3 Additional financial information The book value of Kerin Co's $0·50 ordinary shares is $375m.These shares are currently trading at $5·28 and the finance director expects the price earnings(PE)ratio to increase by 10%if the acquisition proceeds. Danton Co upgraded its main manufacturing facility during the previous year and expects to make annual pre-tax cost savings of $2·5m from the start of the current financial year.The book value of Danton Co's $0·25 ordinary shares is $35m.Based on an analysis of companies of a comparable size and cost structure,it is estimated that Danton Co's PE ratio is 20%higher than Kerrin Co's current PE ratio. Kerrin Co's chief executive officer estimates annual pre-tax revenue and cost synergies of $15·2m to arise as a result of the acquisition.In addition,the finance director anticipates annual pre-tax financial synergies of $5·3m although she insists this is a cautious estimate after reading an article on recent merger and acquisition activity where post-acquisition synergies have either been overestimated or failed to materialise. The rate of corporation tax relevant to both companies is 20%. 题干解析： 表格为两家公司近期财务数据。 其他财务信息： Kerrin 公司普通股每股面值为$0.5,股本为$375m。 这些股票目前的交易价格为$5.28, 并且财务总监预计如果收购继续进行，市盈率将增加10%。 Danton 公司在上一年升级了其主要生产设施，预计从本年度开始，每年税前成本节省 了$2.5m。Danton 公司普通股每股面值为$0.25,股本为$35m。 根据对具有可比规模和 成本结构的分析，估计 Danton 的 PE ratio比 Kerrin Co的 PE ratio高20%。 Kerrin Co 的首席执行官估计，收购会导致每年税前收入和成本的协同效应共计达到 $15.2m 。此外，财务总监预计年度税前财务协同效应为$5.3m, 她坚持认为这是一个谨 慎的估计收购后的协同效应，尽管她最近在阅读了一篇关于近期并购活动的文章，其中 说明收购后协同效应要么被高估要么未能实现。 与两家公司相关的公司税税率为20%。 (a)Discuss possible sources of financial synergy arising from Kerrin Co's acquisition of Danton Co and comment on the finance director's concern that synergy is often overestimated,including any steps which could be taken by Kerrin Co's board to address this problem. (8 marks) Requirements解析： 讨论 Kerrin Co收购 Danton Co 产生的财务协同效应的可能来源，并发表评论财务总监 担心协同效应经常被高估，包括 Kerrin公司董事为解决这一问题可能会采取的措施。 Marking Scheme: (a) Financial synergies Overestimation of synergies Proposed steps 答案： (a)Financial synergies Many acquisitions are justified on the basis that the combined organisation will be more profitable or grow at a faster rate than the companies operating independently.The expectation is that the acquisition will generate higher expected cash flows or a lower cost of capital,creating value for shareholders.The additional value created is known as synergy,the sources of which can be categorised into three types:revenue,cost and financial synergies. Based on the scenario,there are a number of possible sources of financial synergy.As a private company,Danton Co is experiencing a funding constraint whereas Kerin Co has significant cash reserves but limited growth opportunities.The combination of the two can create additional value since Danton Co may be able to utilise Kerrin Co's cash resources to fund its expansion in a way which would not have been possible otherwise,leading to an increase in the expected cash flows. Assuming both companies'cash flows are less than perfectly corelated,those of the combined company will be less volatile than the individual companies operating independently.This reduction in volatility enables the combined company to borrow more and possibly cheaper financing than would otherwise have been possible.This increase in debt capacity,and therefore the present value of the tax shield,increases the value of the combined company in the form of a lower cost of capital. Further benefits may arise if Kerrin Co is able to utilise Danton Co's unrelieved tax losses.Whilst Danton is no longer loss making and could offset these tax losses independently,the combined company may be able to obtain tax relief earlier since the acquisition increases the availability of profits against which carried forward tax losses can be offset.The present value of the tax saved will therefore be greater in the combined company. If both companies were publicly traded,there would be no benefit from diversification since investors are capable of diversifying at a lower cost and with greater ease than the company.However,Danton Co is privately owned and the shareholders are therefore exposed to diversifiable unsystematic risk.Therefore the acquisition may lead to potential diversification and risk reduction benefits.The reduction in the cost of the capital increases the value of the combined company. Overestimation of synergy value There is evidence that bidding companies often overestimate the value of synergy arising from a potential acquisition with the result that companies pay too much for their target.When this happens,there is destruction in wealth for the bidding company's shareholders.There are a number of possible explanations for this problem. First,merger and acquisition activity tends to be driven by the availability of cheap credit.At the peak of a wave of activity,there may be competition for targets,thereby increasing acquisition premiums. Second,conflicts of interest may lead to a biased evaluation process.Deal advisers such as investment banks earn a large proportion of their fees from mergers and acquisitions.Their advice on whether an acquisition makes sense is potentially biased if they do not look after their clients'interests. Third,management overconfidence may explain why this occurs.Acquiring companies may overestimate the acquisition synergy and/or underestimate the timeit will take to deliver.Management may then be reluctant to admit mistakes when the facts change,even when there is still time to back out of a deal.Agency costs may be also a factor if managers are more interested in pursuing personal goals than maximising shareholder wealth. Finally,there may be dificulties integrating the companies due to different work cultures and conflicts of interest. Steps to address this problem Kerin Co's board needs to plan for synergy and take active steps to ensure that it is delivered.This responsibility needs to be allocated to someone who can ensure spare cash is utilised to invest in new growth opportunities,that tax losses are offset as efficiently as possible and that the combined company avails itself of cheaper financing.Companies which allocate this responsibility and monitor and review performance tend to be more successful in creating value.In order to avoid any bias,the deal advisers who stand to profit from an acquisition need to be separate from the evaluation process.Effective due diligence ensures the financial documents which form the basis of a valuation are scrutinised and inspected. 答案解析： 财务协同效应： 许多收购的原因都是因为合并后的公司盈利能力更强或者增长速度更快，相比于独立运 营的公司。预期收购将产生更高的现金流或降低资本成本，从而为股东创造价值。创造 的附加价值被称为协同效应，可以分为三种类型：收入、成本和财务协同效应。 在本案例中，有许多财务协同效应的来源。作为一家私人公司，Danton 公司融资受限， 而 Kerrin Co拥有大量现金储备，但增长机会有限。这两者的结合可以创造额外的价值， 因为Danton 公司可能能够利用 Kerrin 公司的现金资源来实现扩张，这会导致了预期现 金流增加。 假设两家公司的现金流并非完全相关，那么合并后的公司的现金流量波动性就会降低。 波动性的降低使合并后的公司能够募集到更多且更便宜的资金。这会导致企业债务能力 的增加，税盾的现值也会增加，同时增加合并公司的价值，,因为资本成本降低。 如果 Kerrin 公司能够利用Danton 公司未弥补的税收损失，可能会产生更多的收益。虽 然 Danto 不再亏损，但是合并后的公司可以利用 Danton 公司早期的税收损失，因此， 合并后的公司节省的税费会更多。 如果两家公司都是公开交易的，那么分散化不会带来任何好处，因为投资者能够以更低 的成本和更轻松的方式进行分散投资。然而， Danton 公司是私人所有，股东是面临的是 分散的非系统风险。因此，收购可能会带来潜在的多元化并减少风险。资本成本的降低 增加了合并公司的价值。 高估协同价值： 有证据表明，收购方往往高估了潜在收购所产生的协同效应，会导致公司为收购多付出 很多钱。当这种情况发生时，收购方股东的财富会遭到破坏。这个问题有多种可能的解 释。 首先，并购活动往往受到廉价信贷的推动。在收购行为的高峰期，可能有很多竞争对手 从而导致收购方增加收购溢价。 第二，利益冲突可能会导致评估过程存在偏见。投资银行等交易顾问会赚取大额的并购 费用。他们对收购是否合理的建议可能会不公允。 第三，管理层过度自信可以解释为什么会出现这种情况。收购方可能会高估收购协同作 用和/或低估交付所需的时间。管理层可能不愿承认错误，即使还有时间退出交易。如果 管理人员追求的更多的是个人目标而不是最大化股东财富，那么会产生更多的代理成本。 最后，由于不同的工作文化和利益冲突，整合公司可能存在困难。 解决此问题的步骤： Kerrin Co的董事会需要规划协同效应，并采取积极措施确保其能够实现。这种责任需要 分配给能够确保备用现金用于投资新的增长机会，从而更高效的利用税收损失，合并后 的公司可以获得更便宜的融资。公司履行责任，监督和审查绩效往往能更为成功的创造 价值。为了避免任何偏差，从收购中获利的交易顾问需要与估值过程分开。有效尽职调 查，确保对构成估价基础的财务文件是经过审查和检查的。 ( b)Advise the directors on a suitable share-for-share exchange offer which meets the criteria specified by Danton Co's shareholders and calculate the effect of the cash and share-for-share offers on the post-acquisition wealth of both Kerrin Co's and Danton Co's shareholders. (13 marks) Requirements解析： 就符合Danton 股东要求的标准的向董事会建议适合的以股换股方案，并计算现金和股 份交换要约对收购后Kerrin Co 和 Danton Co的股东财富的影响。 Marking Scheme: (b) Kerrin PE ratio Danton valuation Post-acquisition valuation Share-for-share offer terms Advice Impact on shareholder wealth 答案： (b)Pre-acquisition valuations Kerrin Co number of shares =375m/0·5=750m Kerrin Co market value =750mx$5·28=$3,960m Future maintainable earnings(FME)=($381 ·9)×0·8=$305·5m Price earnings(PE)ratio=$3,960m/$305·5m =12·96 Danton Co future maintainable earnings=($116·3m+$2·5m)x0·8=$95·0m Danton Co PE ratio=12·96×1 ·20=15·55 Danton Co PE valuation=15-55×$95m=$1,477·3m Combined Co pre-acquisition valuation=$3,960m+$1,477·3m =$5,437·3m Post-acquisition valuation including synergies Combined Co FME=$305-5m +$95m +($20·5m×0·8)=$416·9m Combined Co PE ratio 12·96×1 ·1=14·3 Combined Co post-merger valuation =14-3x$416·9m=$5,961-7m Value created based on synergies=$5,961·7m-$5,437·3m=$524-4m Share-for-share offer Pre-acquisition valuation Add premium($1,477 ·3mx0 ·3) Balance of excess value to Kerrin Co: $524-4m-$443 ·2m Post-merger valuation Relative valuation Kerrin Co $m 3,960 ·0 81 ·2 4,041 ·2 2 ·1 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Danton Co $m 1,477 ·3 443 ·2 1,920 ·5 1 Kerrin Co new share issue=750m/2·1=357·14m Number of existing Danton Co shares=35mx4=140m Therefore share-for-share offer=357·14/140,i.e.approximately 18 Kerrin Co shares for every 7 Danton Co old shares Advice on terms of share-for-share offer Danton Co shareholders would receive 140mx(18/7)=360m new Kerrin Co shares. Total Kerrin Co shares=750m+360m =1,110m. Kerrin Co shareholders own 67·6%(750m/1,110m)and Danton Co shareholders 32·4%(360m/1,110m)of the post-acquisition company. Impact on shareholder wealth Pre-acquisition valuation Cash offer Danton Co shareholders cash received: $13 ·10×140m shares Kerrin Co post-acquisition equity valuation: $5,961 ·7m less acquisition cost of $1,834 ·0m Increase in shareholder wealth Share-for-share offer Post-acquisition value Kerrin Co:(750/1,110)x$5,961-7 Danton Co(360/1,110)x$5,961-7 Increase in shareholder wealth Kerrin Co $m 3,960 ·0 4,127 ·7 4 ·2% 4,028 ·2 1 ·7% Danton Co $m 1,477 ·3 1,834 ·0 24-1% 1,933 ·5 30-9% The terms of the share-for-share offer meet the criteria specified by Danton Co's directors. (c) Discuss the likely reaction of Kerrin Co's and Danton Co's shareholders to the cash and share-for-share offers. (4 marks) Requirements解析： 就符合 Danton 规定标准的适当股份交换要约向董事提供建议公司股东，并计算现金对 价和以股换股对收购后 Kerrin Co和 Danton Co股东财富的影响。 Marking Scheme: Share-for-share offer 2-3(c) Cash offer 2-3 Share-for-share offer 2-3 Max 4 答案： (c)Cash offer The main advantage of a cash offer is that it provides Danton Co's shareholders with a certain and immediate return.However, the premium is lower compared to the share-for-share offer and may be reduced even further if the realised gain gives rise to a tax liability.By indicating their preferred premium under both offers,it is possible the shareholders have priced in the risks associated with an uncertain share-for-share offer and on this basis may be indifferent between the two.The cash offer may give rise to agency issues since Danton Co's founders no longer have a stake in the business even though Kerrin Co's board is keen to ensure the founders remain in position after the acquisition.The information provided is too limited to read too much into the intentions of the venture capitalist.However,typically a venture capitalist would be expected to exit within three to five years.In this case,they may prefer the certainty of the cash offer. The cash offer transfers more of the added value to Kerin Co without the need fordilution,which may appeal to the shareholders. As indicated in the question,Kerrin Co's existing reserves are sufficient to fund the cash offer although this may constrain future dividends and/or investment decisions. Share-for-share offer Both sets of shareholders benefit from increased wealth as a result of the share offer,albeit only marginally so in the case of Kerrin Co's shareholders.Another drawback is that Kerrin Co's shareholders'percentages are also diluted under this method. However,a share-for-share offer would ensure that Danton Co's founders'interests are aligned with Kerrin Co's shareholders, reducing possible agency costs.It also provides Danton Co's shareholders with the right to participate in the future growth of the larger company,which the cash offer would prevent. No basis has been provided for the synergistic benefits;the increase in shareholder wealth is so marginal even a minor deviation from the estimates could result in a reduction in shareholder wealth for the owners of Kerrin Co.On this basis,it is quite likely they will not approve a share-for-share offer without further negotiation around the acquisition premium. ( Note:Credit will be given for alternative and valid comments) 答案解析： 现金对价： 现金对价的主要优势是为 Danton 公司的股东提供了一定的即时回报。但是，与以股换 股相比溢价更低，并且如果实现的收益要交税的话获得的溢价会更低。通过计算这两种 报价下的溢价，股东有可能对不确定的以股换股的风险进行了定价，在此基础上，两者 之间可能无所谓。现金报价可能会产生代理问题，因为Danton 的创始人不再拥有该公 司的股份，计算Kerrin 公司的董事希望创始人在收购后能继续留任。关于风投公司提供 的信息太有限，无法看出其意图。然而，风投公司通常会在三到五年内退出。在这种情 况下，他们可能更喜欢现金报价的确定性。 现金要约将更多的附加值转移给 Kerrin Co,无需稀释。这可能会吸引股东。如题中所示， Kerrin Co的现金储备足以为现金收购提供资金，尽管这可能会限制未来股息和/或投资 决策。 以股换股： 这两组股东都从股票发行带来的财富增加中受益，尽管 Kerrin Co 公司的股东仅仅只有 微薄的增长。另一个缺点是，Kerrin Co原股东的持股比率也在这种方法下被稀释。 然而，以股换股的要约将确保Danton Co 创始人的利益与Kerrin Co的股东一致，减少 可能的代理成本。它还为Danton Co的股东提供了参与合并公司未来增长的权利，这一 点现金收购无法实现。 题中对于协同效益没有提供依据。股东财富的增加微不足道，仅仅与估计值的小幅偏差 就可能会导致Kerrin 公司所有者的股东财富减少。因此很有可能，在没有围绕收购溢价 进行进一步谈判的情况下，他们不会批准以股换股的要约。 Introduction Westparley Co is a listed retailer,mainly selling food and small household goods.It has outperformed its competitors over the last few years as a result of providing high quality products at reasonable prices,and also having a stronger presence online.It has kept a control on costs, partly by avoiding operating large stores on expensive city centre sites.Instead,it has had smaller stores on the edge of cities and towns,and a limited number of larger stores on convenient out-of- town sites,aiming at customers who want their journeys to shops to be quick.One of its advertising slogans has been:'We are where you want us to be.' Westparley Co's share price has recently performed better than most companies in the retail sector generally.Share prices in the retail sector have been relatively low as a result of poor results due to high competition,large fixed cost base and high interest rates.The exception has been shares in retailers specialising in computer and high-technology goods.These shares appear to have benefited from a boom generally in share prices of high-technology companies.Some analysts believe share prices of many companies in the high-technology sector are significantly higher than a rational analysis of their future prospects would indicate. 题干解析： Westparley Co是一家上市零售商，主要销售食品和家用商用。过去几年，由于合理的价 格，高质量的产品，并且在网上拥有更强大的影响力，该公司的表现优于竞争对手。它一 直避免在昂贵的市中心经营大型商店以控制成本。它在城市边缘有较小的商店，在城外有 较大的店，目标是那些希望快速购物的顾客。它的广告之一是：“我们就在你想要的地 方。” Westparley Co的股价最近表现好于零售行业的大多数公司。由于竞争激烈、固定成本基 数大和利率高，零售业的股价一直相对较低。例外的是专门从事计算机和高科技商品的零 售商的股票。这些股票似乎受益于高科技公司股价的普遍上涨。一些分析师认为，许多高 科技行业公司的股价明显高于对其未来前景的理性分析所显示的水平。 Matravers Co Westparley Co has identified the listed retailer Matravers Co as an acquisition target,because it believes that Matravers Co's shares are currently undervalued and part of Matravers Co's operations would be a good strategic fit for Westparley Co. Matravers Co operates two types of store: Matravers Home mainly sells larger household items and home furnishings.These types of retailer have performed particularly badly recently and one major competitor of Matravers Home has just gone out of business.Matravers Home operates a number of city centre sites but has a much higher proportion of out-of-town sites than its competitors. Matravers Tech sells computers and mobile phones in much smaller outlets than those of Matravers Home. 题干解析： Westparley Co已将上市零售商Matravers Co确定为收购目标，因为该公司认为 Matraverss Co的股票目前被低估，Matraverse Co的部分业务非常适合Westparey Co的发 展战略。 Matravers Co经营两种类型的商店： Matravers Home主要销售大型家居用品和家具。这些类型的零售商最近表现特别糟糕， Matravers Home的一个主要竞争对手刚刚倒闭。Matravers Home经营着多个市中心商 店，但与竞争对手相比，城外商店的比例要高得多。 Matravers Tech在比Matraverss Home小得多的专营店销售电脑和手机。 Extracts from Matravers Co's latest annual report are given below: Pre-tax profit Long-term loan Share capital ($1 shares) $m 1,950 6,500 5,000 The share of pre-tax profit between Matravers Home and Matravers Tech was 80:20. The current market value of Matravers Co's shares is $12,500m and its debt is currently trading at its book value.Westparley Co believes that it will have to pay a premium of 15%to Matravers Co's shareholders to buy the company. Westparley Co intends to take advantage of the current values attributed to businesses such as Matravers Tech by selling this part of Matravers Co at the relevant sector price earnings ratio of 18,rather than a forecast estimate of Matravers Tech's present value of future free cash flows of $4,500m. The company tax rate for both companies is 28%per year 题干解析： Matravers Co最新年度报告摘录如表 Matravers Home和Matravers Tech之间的税前利润比例为80:20。 Matravers Co股票的当前市值为$12,500m, 其债务目前以账面价值交易。Westparley Co 认为，要收购该公司，必须向Matravers公司的股东支付15%的溢价。 Westparley Co打算利用相关行业市盈率18出售Matravers Tech, 而不是用Matravers Tech未来现金流现的现值$4,500m出售。 两家公司的税率均为每年28%。 Post-acquisition cost of capital The post-acquisition cost of capital of the combined company will be based on its cost of equity and cost of debt.The asset beta post-acquisition can be assumed to be both companies'asset betas weighted in proportion to their current market value of equity. Westparley Co has 4,000 million $1 shares in issue,currently trading at $8.50.It has $26,000m debt in issue,currently trading at$105 per$100 nominal value.Its equity beta is 1.02. Matravers Co's asset beta is 0.75.The current market value of Matravers Co's shares is $12,500m and its long-term loan is currently trading at its book value of $6,500m. The risk-free rate of return is estimated to be 3.5%and the market risk premium is estimated to be 8%. The pre-tax cost of debt of the combined company is expected to be 9.8%.It can be assumed that the debt:equity ratio of the combined company will be the same as Westparley Co's current debt:equity ratio in market values. The company tax rate for both companies is 28%per year. 题干解析： 收购后资本成本 合并公司的收购后资本成本将基于其股权成本和债务成本。收购后的资产贝塔值可以假设 为两家公司的资产贝塔值与其收购前股权市值成比例加权。 Westparley Co发行了4,000 m股每股$1的股票，目前交易价为$8.50。发行了$26,000m 的 债券，目前交易价格为每$105面值$100。其股本贝塔值为1.02。 Matravers Co的资产贝塔值为0.75。Matraverss Co股票的当前市值为$12,500m, 其长期 贷款目前以$6,500m 的账面价值交易。 无风险回报率估计为3.5%,市场风险溢价为8%。 合并公司的税前债务成本预计为9.8%。可以假设合并后的公司的债务：股权比率将与 Westparley 公司的当前债务：股权市值比率相同。 两家公司的税率均为每年28%。 Plans for Matravers Co The offer for Matravers Co will be a cash offer.Any funding required for this offer willbe a mixture of debt and equity.Although for the purposes of the calculation it has been assumed that the overall mix of debt and equity will remain the same,the directors are considering various plans for funding the purchase which could result in a change in Westparley Co's gearing. As soon as it acquires all of Matravers Co's share capital,Westparley Co would sell Matravers Tech as it does not fit in with Westparley Co's strategic plans and Westparley Co wishes to take advantage of the large values currently attributed to high-technology businesses.Westparley Co would then close Matravers Home's worst-performing city centre stores.It anticipates the loss of returns from these stores would be partly compensated by higher online sales by Matravers Co, generated by increased investment in its online operations.The remaining city centre stores and all out-of-town stores would start selling the food and household items currently sold in Westparley Co's stores,and Westparley Co believes that this would increase profits from those stores. Westparley Co also feels that reorganising Matravers Co's administrative functions and using increased power as a larger retailer can lead to synergies after the acquisition. 题干解析： 收购Matravers Co将是现金报价。这项提议所需的任何资金都将是债务和股权的混合。出 于计算的目的，假设债务和股权的总体组合将保持不变，但董事会正在考虑为收购提供资 金的各种计划，这可能会导致Westparley Co的负债率发生变化。 一旦收购了Matravers Co的全部股本，Westparley Co将出售Matraverss Tech,因 为 它 不 符合Westparly Co的战略计划，并且Westparey Co希望利用目前高科技企业的巨大价值。 Westparley Co随后将关闭Matravers Home表现最差的市中心商店。该公司预计，关闭这 些商店的损失将通过增加在线运营投资提高在线销售额得到部分补偿，剩下的市中心商店 和所有城外商店将开始出售目前在Westparley Co商店出售的食品和家居用品，Westparey Co 相信这将增加这些商店的利润。 Westparley Co还认为，重组Matravers Co的行政部门，并作为一家更大的零售商获得更 大的议价权力，可以在收购后产生协同效应。 Post-acquisition details Once Matravers Tech has been sold,Westparley Co estimates that sales revenue from the Matravers Home stores which remain open,together with the online sales from its home business, will be $43,260m in the first year post-acquisition,and this figure is expected to grow by 3%per year in years 2 to 4. The profit margin before interest and tax is expected to be 6%of sales revenue in years 1 to 4. Tax allowable depreciation is assumed to be equivalent to the amount of investment needed to maintain existing operations.However,an investment in assets (including working capital)will be required of $630m in year 1.In years 2 to 4,investment in assets each year willbe $0.50 of every $1 increase in sales revenue. After four years,the annual growth rate of free cash flows is expected to be 2%for the foreseeable future. As well as the free cash flows from Matravers Co,Westparley Co expects that post-tax synergies will arise from its planned reorganisation of Matravers Co as follows in the next three years: Year 1 2 3 Free cash flows $m 700 $m $m 750 780 The current market value of Matravers Co's shares is $12,500m and its debt is currently trading at its book value of $6,500m. 题干解析： 一旦Matravers Tech被出售，Westparley Co估计，在收购后的第一年，Matravers Home 门店的销售收入以及在线销售额将达到$43,260m, 预计这一数字将在第2至第4年以每年 3%的速度增长。 预计第1年至第4年的息税前利润率为销售收入的6%。 Tax allowable depreciation假设等于维持现有运营所需的投资额。然而，第一年的资产投 资(包括营运资本)将需要$630m 。在第二至第四年，每增加的$1销售收入，每年的资产 投资将为$0.50。 四年后，在可预见的未来，现金流的年增长率预计为2%。 除了Matravers Co的现金流外，Westparley Co预计，未来三年，其计划重组Matraverss Co 将产生税后协同效应，具体如表： Matravers Co股份的当前市值为$12,500m, 其债务目前以$6,500m 的账面价值交易。 Requirement (a)Discuss the behavioural factors which may have led to businesses such as Matravers Tech being valued highly. Requirements解析： 分析可能导致Matravers Tech这类企业被高估的因素。 Marking Scheme (a) 1-2 marks per relevant point (examples may include asking price,opportunity to purchase and information available is positive,herd instinct,following fashion and confirmation bias) Max 6 答案： Individual business A number of behavioural factors,to do with the individual company as well as the sector as a whole,may lead to Matravers Tech being valued higher than appears to be warranted by rational analysis of its future prospects.One possible factor is the asking price,even if it is not a fair one, may provide a reference point which significantly influences the purchaser's valuation of the business. The fact that Matravers Tech is available for purchase may help raise its price.Purchasers may see this as a rare opportunity to buy an attractive business in this retail sector.This will be made more likely if investors have loss aversion bias,a desire to buy Matravers Tech now because otherwise the opportunity will be lost. Matravers Tech being offered for sale will mean that information about the company,showing it in a positive light,will be available for purchasers.This could result in availability bias,investors taking particular note of this information because they can readily obtain it,rather than other information which may be more difficult or costly to find. Sector There are a number of possible behavioural reasons why share prices in this sector appear generally higher than rational analysis indicates.One is the herd instinct,investing in the sector because other investors have also been buying shares,not wishing to make judgements independently of other investors. The herd instinct may be generated by previous share price movements.Investors may believe once prices start rising in the sector,they will continue to do so indefinitely. Following fashion may also be a factor.Fund managers who wish to give the impression that they are actively managing their portfolio by making regular changes to it,may have a preference for companies which appear up-to-date and are currently popular.This may be linked to an expectation that sales of technologically-advanced goods are likely to generate high returns. There is also confirmation bias,the idea that investors will pay attention to evidence which confirms their views that the sector is a good one in which to invest,and ignore evidence which contradicts their beliefs.In the past,technology companies have been valued using methods which support the beliefs of investors that they are of high value,rather than traditional methods,such as cash flow analysis,which suggest a lower business value is more realistic. 答案解析： 单个公司 单个公司以及整个行业都有许多行为因素可能导致Matravers Tech的估值高于对其未来前 景的理性分析所证明的估值。其中一个可能的因素是卖方要价，即使它不公允，也是显著 影响购买者对企业的估值一个参考。 Matravers Tech出售事实可能有助于提高其价格。购买者可能会认为这是一个难得的机 会，可以在这个零售行业购买一个有吸引力的业务。如果投资者有规避损失的偏好，那么 就会希望现在就购买Matravers Tech,否则机会将失去这种机会。 Matravers Tech将被出售，这意味着有关该公司的信息，会提供给购买者。这可能会导致 可用性偏差，投资者特别注意这些信息，因为他们很容易获得这些信息，而不是其他可能 更难或更昂贵的信息。 行业 这一行业的股价普遍高于理性分析的价格，可能有许多原因。 一种是羊群效应，因为其他 投资者也在购买该行业股票，不希望独立于其他投资者做出判断。羊群效应可能是由之前 的股价波动产生的。投资者可能相信， 一旦该行业的价格开始上涨，他们将无限期地继续 上涨。 追随潮流也可能是一个因素。如果基金经理希望通过定期更改投资组合来给人一种积极管 理投资组合的印象，他们可能会倾向于投资那些看起来最新、最受欢迎的公司。这可能与 技术先进商品的销售可能产生高回报的预期有关。 还有一种偏见，即投资者会关注证实他们认为该行业是一个值得投资的好行业的证据，而 忽略与他们的信念相矛盾的证据。过去，科技公司的估值方式支持投资者的高价值信念， 而不是传统方法，如现金流分析，后者表明较低的估值更为现实。 (b)Prepare a report for the board of directors of Westparley Co which: (i)compares the additional value which Westparley Co believes can be generated from the sale of Matravers Tech based on the P/E ratio,with that of the projected present value of its future free cash flows; (ii)calculates the weighted average cost of capital for the combined company; (iii)estimates the total value which Westparley Co's shareholders will gain from the acquisition of Matravers Co;and (iv)assesses the strategic and financial value to Westparley Co of the acquisition,including a discussion of the estimations and assumptions made. Professional marks will be awarded in part(b)for the format,structure and presentation of the report. Requirements解析： 为Westparley 公司董事会准备一份报告，该报告包括以下内容： (i) 将Westparley Co 认为基于市盈率出售 Matravers Tech 可产生的附加价值与其未来 自由现金流的预计现值进行比较； (ii)计算合并公司的加权平均资本成本； (iii) 估计Westparley 公司股东从收购 Matravers 公司中获得的总价值；和 (iv) 评估收购对Westparley 公司的战略和财务价值，包括对所做估计和假设的讨论。 报告的格式、结构和表述将在(b) 部分获得专业分。 Marking Scheme (b)(i) Share of pre-tax profit After-tax profit Proceeds from sell-off Comparison with free cash flow valuation 1 1 1 1 工 工 4 (ii) Westparley Co MV debt and equity Westparley Co asset beta 1 1111Combined company asset beta 1 1 1 1 Combined company equity beta Combined company cost of equity Combined company cost of capital 6 (iii )Sales revenue Profit before interest and tax Tax Additional capital investment PV of free cash flows years 1-4 PV of free cash flows year 5 onwards Present value of synergies Premi um payable Value attributable to Westparley Co's shareholders 2 l1 l 1 1 1 10 (iv) Strategic value Financial value Estimations made Assumptions made Max Professional marks for part(b) Report format Structure and presentation of the report 3-4 2-3 3-43-4 3-4 12 l 3 l 3 4 答 案 ： (iv)Report to the board of directors,Westparley Co This report evaluates whether the acquisition of Matravers Co would be beneficial to Westparley Co's shareholders by estimating the future value generated by Matravers Co(i.e.Matravers Home currently),the proceeds from selling Matravers Tech and the additional value created from synergies immediately after the companies are combined. Strategic fit The strategic case for taking over the business appears to be strongest for the out-of-town stores and the online business.The acquisition would provide an additional out-of-town presence for Westparley Co.Better usage in the out-of-town stores could generate higher returns.Having the food and home businesses on the same site could generate some cross-sales between the two. Possibly combining the two companies'online presence and investing further could mean Matravers Home benefiting from the factors which have driven strong performance by Westparley Co. Taking over the city centre stores,even the successful ones,seems to have less strategic logic, however.Westparley Co would be taking on a high cost burden.The success of the food business in city centres is doubtful,as food shops sited there will be less convenient for customers who do not live in the city centres,and Westparley Co has marketed itself as being easily accessible for customers.There is,perhaps,wider incompatibility between the two businesses.The food business is characterised by quick shopping for often a limited number of items,whereas purchases in the home business,particularly of larger items,are likely to take longer and site convenience be less of an issue. Financial aspects Based on the predictions for future cash flows and required premiums from Matravers Co's shareholders,the acquisition would add value to Westparley Co's shareholders,if,and only if,the excess value on selling Matravers Tech and the synergies are both largely achieved.Together they add up to $2,400m($558m+$1,842m)compared with total added value of$1,897m.There are questions about the estimates for these figures and also the estimates for the future free cash flows of the current Matravers Home business. Synergies Most of the additional value is due to synergies and it is difficult to see how the synergies are calculated.There is likely to be scope for some administrative savings.However,operational cost synergies appear less obvious as the two companies are operating in different retail sectors.Any synergy figures will also have to take account of costs in achieving synergies,such as store closure costs,and also commitments such as leases which may be a burden for some time. Synergies may also not be achieved because of lack of co-operation by staffor problems integrating the two businesses. Current Matravers Home business The suggested increase in cash flows appears doubtful for a number of reasons.If stores being closed are making positive cash flow contributions,these will have to be replaced.Whether they can be is doubtful given the problems in this part ofthe retail sector.It may be a more profitable use of store space to have an area for food sales,but the food sales generated in Matravers Co's shops may take business from Westparley Co's existing shops.Similarly,increased online sales may be at the expense of sales in stores. Sale of Matravers Tech There is no indication of how interested buyers will be in the business.The industry price-earnings (P/E)ratio used may be an average which does not reflect Matravers Tech's circumstances.It would be better to find a P/E ratio for a proxy company with similar financial and business risk. As Matravers Tech would not be listed,this would suggest a discount to the P/E ratio should be applied.Since also Westparley Co has an estimate of future free cash flow,potential buyers may be able to come up with their own estimates and base the price they are prepared to pay on their estimates. Other assumptions One important assumption is the 15%premium expected to be required by Matravers Co's shareholders.Other assumptions made in the calculations include operating profit margin and tax rates remaining constant and cash flows being assumed to increase to perpetuity.Incremental capital investment is assumed to be accurate.It is assumed that the cost of debt will remain unchanged and that the asset beta,cost of equity and cost of debt can be determined accurately. Given all the assumptions,Westparley Co should carry out sensitivity analysis using different assumptions and obtaining a range of values. Conclusion On the assumptions made,the acquisition appears to add financial value for the shareholders of Westparley Co.However,the figures are subject to a significant number of uncertainties and the strategic logic for buying the whole Matravers business appears unclear.On balance,Westparley Co may want to consider a more limited acquisition of just the out-of-town stores if these are available,as their acquisition appears to make better strategic sense. =$15,872m+$5,058m+$1,842m-$20,875m=$1,897m 答 案 解 析 ： 注意所有的计算在report 中需要以Appendix 的形式列示在 report后面(机考模式中，在 excel 中列示所有的计算，在word 中进行 report。Report 中引用的数据建议说明清楚来自 哪 个appendix 。) 董事会报告 本报告通过估算 Matravers Co (即目前的 Matraverss Home) 产生的未来价值、出售 MatraversTech 的收益以及公司合并后立即产生的协同效应产生的额外价值，评估收购 MatraversCo是否对 Westparley Co 的股东有利。 战略契合度 收购该业务的战略理由似乎对城外商店和在线业务最为有力。此次收购将为Westparley Co. 提供更多的城外业务。更好地利用在城外商店可以产生更高的回报。将食品和家居企业放在 同一个网站上可能会在两者之间产生一些交叉销售。结合两家公司的在线业务和进一步投 资，可能意味着 Matravers Home 将成为推动 Westparley 公司业绩的因素。 但是，接管市中心的商店，即使是成功的商店，似乎也没有那么具有战略逻辑。Westparley 公司将承担高昂的成本负担。市中心的食品商店能否成功令人怀疑，因为那里的食品店对不 住在市中心的顾客来说不太方便，而且Westparley Co 将自己营销为顾客很便利。这两家公 司之间可能存在更广泛的不兼容。食品行业的特点是快速购物，通常购买数量有限的物品， 而在家居行业购买，尤其是购买较大的物品，可能需要更长的时间，而且商店便利性问题较 小 。 财务方面 根据Matravers Co对未来现金流的预测和股东所需premiums, 如果且仅当出售Matraverss Tech 的超额价值和协同效应基本实现，收购将为Westparley Co股东增加价值。超额价值 和协同效应分别为$558m,$1,842m 共$2,400m, 股东价值总增加值$1,897m。对于这些数 字及当前 Matravers Home 未来自由现金流的估计存在疑问。 协同作用 大部分增加的价值是由于协同作用，但是很难看出协同作用是如何计算的。可能会有一些行 政费用节省。然而，由于两家公司在不同的零售行业运营，运营成本协同效应似乎不那么明 显。任何数字还必须考虑实现协同效应的成本，如商店关闭成本，以及租赁等已签协议的支 出。由于员工缺乏合作或两类业务的整合问题，也可能导致协同效应无法实现。 Matravers Home 由于一些原因，预计的现金流增长似乎令人怀疑。如果被关闭的商店为现金流做出了积极贡 献，则需要有替代这些商店的业务。考虑到零售行业的问题，他们是否能做到这件事值得怀 疑。在Matravers Home设立一个食品销售区域可能会有效的利用商店空间，但 Matravers Co 的商店的食品销售可能会夺走Westparley Co现有的商店的生意。同样，在线销售的增 加可能会以降低商店销售为代价 出售 Matravers Tech 没有迹象表明有买家对这部分业务感兴趣。使用的行业市盈率 (P/E) 可能是一个平均值， 不能反映 Matravers Tech 的情况。最好从具有类似财务和商业风险的公司找市盈率。由于 Matravers Tech 不会上市，这意味着应该对市盈率进行折扣。由于Westparley Co 有未来 自由现金流的估值，潜在买家也能够自行估计，并根据估值确定他们准备支付的价格。 其他假设 一个重要的假设是 Matravers Co的股东预计需要15%的溢价。其他假设包括运营利润率和 税率保持不变，以及假设现金流持续增长，增量资本投资是准确的，债务成本保持不变，资 产贝塔、权益成本和债务成本可以准确预测。Westparley 公司应对不同的假设进行敏感性 分析，并获得一系列值。 结论 根据所做的假设，收购似乎为Westparley Co.的股东增加了财务价值。然而，这些数字存在 大量不确定性，收购整个Matravers 业务的战略逻辑似乎不清楚。总的来说，如果市外商店 可用，Westparley Co 可能会考虑更有限地收购这些商店，因为这类收购似乎更有战略意义。 (c)Discuss the factors which may determine how the offer for Matravers Co will be financed and hence the level of gearing which Westparley Co will have. Requirements解析： 讨论可能决定收购Matravers Co将如何融资即Westparley Co将拥有的杠杆水平的因素。 Marking Scheme (c) 1-2 marks per relevant point (examples may include available security,available tax relief,shareholder attitude to debt, industry norms) Max 8 Total 50 答案： Calculation of gearing If gearing is calculated on the basis of market values,a fall in the share price will result in the level of gearing rising.Westparley Co's board may be worried about a fall in the share price given the problems affecting many companies'share prices in the retail sector and the possibility that the stock market may react adversely to the acquisition. Directors'preferences Directors may have their own preferences about financing.They may be able to choose a mix of sources and a level of gearing which reflects these preferences.Directors may be concerned about too high a burden of payment to finance providers,in terms of cost or ultimately repayment of debt.They may not wish to commit the company to conditions imposed by finance providers. By contrast,they may be concerned about how a change in the shareholder base as a result of a share issue may impact upon their own position.Directors may also be concerned about the impression given by their choice of finance.Pecking order theory states that equity issue is seen as the last resort for financing,so if the purchase is financed by an equity issue,it may be seen as a sign of a lack of confidence by directors that Westparley Co can sustain its current share price. Costs and cash flows Gearing decisions may not just be determined by their own preferences but by external conditions or constraints.Choosing more debt could lower the overall cost of capital,due to lower cost and tax relief,making investments such as Matravers Co appear more profitable.Higher levels of debt may result in the cost of equity rising,reducing the overall impact on the cost of capital.Against that,higher levels of debt mean increased finance cost commitments,even though Westparley Co may need further cash for investment in stores.This may be an important concern if interest rates are high.By contrast,dividends to shareholders do not have to be paid when returns are low or money is required for investment,although failure to meet dividend expectations may result in the board being pressurised by shareholders. Availability The availability of finance may also be a significant issue,particularly if an acquisition has to be completed quickly.An equity issue may take time to arrange and require shareholder approval. Sufficient debt finance may be difficult to obtain if lenders feel that Westparley Co already has significant commitments to debt finance providers.The timescale over which finance is available may be significant.Westparley Co may seek longer-term finance if existing debt finance is due to be repaid soon or if significant cash is needed for short-term investment,not just in Matravers Co's stores,but also in Westparley Co's existing stores. Mix Other external factors may influence the mix of finance chosen.Westparley Co's directors may be concerned about keeping the level of gearing at or below the industry average,because of finance providers becoming worried if gearing exceeds industry levels.Keeping debt as a significant element in overall finance may act as a deterrent to acquirers becoming interested in making a bid for Westparley Co.Directors may also not have a target figure in mind but be content if gearing is within a range of values. 答案解析： 计算杠杆率 如果根据市值计算杠杆率，股价下跌将导致杠杆率水平上升。Westparley 公司董事会可能 担心股价下跌会导致类似售行业其他公司相似的问题，以及收购可能对估价产生不利影响。 董事的偏好 董事可能对融资有自己的偏好。他们可能会按偏好去选择资金来源和杠杆水平。董事们可能 会担心，从成本或最终偿还债务的角度来看，向财资金提供者支付的费用过高。他们可能不 希望让公司遵守资金提供方提出的条件。 相比之下，他们可能会担心由于股票发行而导致的股东基础变化会影响自己的地位。董事 也可能担心他们资金偏好给人留下的印象。 Pecking order理论指出，股权发行被视为融资 的最后手段，因此，如果收购是通过股权发行发行股票融资的，这可能被视为董事对 Westparley Co能否维持目前股价缺乏信心的迹象。 成本和现金流 杠杆决策可能不仅取决于他们自己的偏好，还取决于外部条件或约束。由于更低的成本和 税收减免，选择更多的债务可以降低总体资本成本，使得Matravers Co等投资看起来更有 利可图。较高的债务水平可能会导致股本成本上升，从而降低对资本成本的总体影响。更 高的债务水平意味着利息的增加，尽管Westparley 公司可能需要更多的现金来投资商店。 如果利率很高，这可能会是一个重要的问题。相比之下选择股权融资，当回报率低或投资 需要资金时，不必向股东支付股息，尽管未能达到股息预期可能会导致董事会受到股东的 压力。 可利用性 资金的可用性也可能是一个重大问题，尤其是在收购必须迅速完成的情况下。股权发行可 能需要时间来安排并需要股东批准。如果贷款人认为Westparley Co已经有很高负债，那 么很难获得足够的债务融资。资金可用的时间尺度可能很重要。如果已有债务融资即将偿 还，或者短期投资需要大量现金(例如在Matravers Co的门店，而且在Westparley Co的 现有门店),Westparly Co可能会寻求长期融资。 混合 其他外部因素也可能会影响所选择的融资组合。 Westparley Co的董事可能担心将杠杆率 水平保持在行业平均水平或低于行业平均水平，因为如果杠杆率超过行业水平，资金提供 方会感到担忧。提高债务，可能会阻碍收购方有意竞购Westparley Co.。董事们也可能没 有目标数字，但如果杠杆比率在一定范围内，他们会很满意。 Boullain Co is based in the Eurozone and manufactures components for agricultural machinery. The company is financed by a combination of debt and equity,having obtained a listing five years ago.In addition to the founder's equity stake,the shareholders consist of pension funds and other institutional investors.Until recently,sales have been generated exclusively within the Eurozone area but the directors are keen to expand and have identified North America as a key export market.The company recently completed its first sale to a customer based in the United States, although payment will not be received for another six months. 题干解析： Boullain Co总部位于欧元区，生产农业机械零部件。该公司五年前获得上市，由债务和股 权组合融资。除了创始人的股权，股东还包括养老基金和其他机构投资者。直到最近，销 售仅在欧元区内，但董事们热衷于扩张，并将北美确定为主要出口市场。该公司最近完成 了对美国一家客户的首次销售，但是在接下来的六个月内不会收到付款。 Hedging policy and key stakeholders At a recent board meeting,Boullain Co's finance director argued that the expansion into foreign markets creates the need for a formal hedging policy and that shareholder value would be enhanced if this policy was communicated to the company's other stakeholders.However, Boullain Co's chief executive officer disagreed with the finance director on the following grounds. First,existing shareholders are already well diversified and would therefore not benefit from additional risk reduction hedging strategies.Second,there is no obvious benefit to shareholder value by communicating the hedging policy to other stakeholders such as debt providers, employees,customers and suppliers.You have been asked to provide a rationale for the finance director's comments in advance of the next board meeting. 题干解析： 对冲政策和关键利益相关方 在最近的一次董事会会议上， Boullain Co的财务总监称，向外国市场的扩张需要一项正式 的对冲政策，如果将该政策传达给公司的其他利益相关方，股东价值将得到提高。然而， Boullain Co的首席执行官不同意财务总监的意见，理由如下。首先，现有股东已经多元 化，因此不会受益于额外的风险降低对冲策略。其次，通过向其他利益相关方(如债务提 供者、员工、客户和供应商)传达对冲政策，对股东价值没有明显的好处。你被要求在下 次董事会会议之前对财务总监意见提供理由。 Hedging products Assume today's date is 1 March 20X0.Boullain Co is due to receive$18,600,000 from the American customer on 31 August 20X0.The finance director is keen to minimise the company's exposure to foreign exchange risk and has identified forward contracts,exchange traded futures and options as a way of achieving this objective. The following quotations have been obtained. Exchange rates (quoted as €/US$1) Spot Six months forward 0·8707-0·8711 0·8729-0·8744 Currency futures(contract size E200,000;exercise price quoted as USS per €1) Exercise price March 1·1476 June 1·1449 September 1·1422 Currency options(contract size ∈200,000;exercise price quoted as USS per€1,premium:US cents per €1) Exercise 1·1420 price September 0·89 Assume futures and options contracts mature at the month end and that there is no basis risk.The number of contracts to be used should be rounded down to the nearest whole number in calculations.If the full amount cannot be hedged using an exact number of futures or options contracts,the balance is hedged using the forward market. 题干解析： 对冲产品 假设今天的日期是20X0 年3月1日。 Boullain Co将于20X0 年8月31日从美国客户处收到 $18,600,000。财务总监希望将公司的外汇风险降至最低，可以使用远期合约、 exchange traded futures和期权去实现目标。 已获得以下报价。 假设期货和期权合约在月末到期，并且没有基差风险。在计算中，要使用的合同数量应小 数点后舍去到最接近的整数。如果无法使用整数的期货或期权合约对冲全额，则使用远期 合约对冲余额。 Margin information Once the position is open,the euro futures contract outlined above will be marked-to-market on a daily basis.The terms of the contract require Boullain Co to deposit an initial margin of$3,500 per contract with the clearing house.Assume the maintenance margin is equivalent to the initial margin.The tick size on the contract is $0.0001. Your manager is concerned about the impact of an open futures position on Boullain Co's cash flow and has asked you to calculate and explain the impact of the following hypothetical changes in the closing settlement price in the first three days of the contract. Closing settlement Date 1 March 2 March 3 March prices(US$per €1) Settlement price 1·1410 1·1418 1·1433 题干解析： 保证金信息 一旦头寸打开，上述欧元期货合约将每日按市价计价。合同条款要求Boullain Co与清算所 签订的每份合同存入$3,500的初始保证金。假设维护保证金等于初始保证金。合同上的刻 度大小为$0.0001。 您的经理担心未平仓期货头寸对Boullain Co现金流的影响，并要求您计算并解释合同前三 天收盘结算价格变化的影响。 Required: (a)Explain the rationale for the policy of hedging Boullain Co's foreign exchange risk and the potential benefits to shareholder value if that policy is effectively communicated to the company's key stakeholders.(7marks) Requirements解析： 解释 Boullain Co 对冲外汇风险政策的基本原理，以及如果该政策有效传达给公司的主要利 益相关者，对股东价值的潜在好处。 Marking Scheme (a) Rationale for hedging policy Communication of policy with stakeholders Max MarKS 3-4 3-4 7 答 案 ： Rationale for hedging policy Within the framework of Modigliani and Miller,Boullain Co's CEO is correct in stating that a company's hedging policy is irrelevant.In a world without transaction or agency costs,and where markets are efficient and information symmetrical,hedging creates no value if shareholders are well diversified.Shareholder value may even be destroyed if the costs associated with hedging exceed the benefits. However,in the real world where market imperfections exist,including the transaction costs of bankruptcy and other types of financial distress,hedging protects shareholder value by avoiding the distress costs associated with potentially devastating foreign exchange fluctuations. Active hedging may also benefit debt-holders by reducing the agency costs of debt.A clearly defined hedging policy acts as a signalling tool between shareholders and debt-holders.In this sense,hedging allows for higher leverage and a lower cost of debt and reduces the need for restrictive covenants. Communication of policy with stakeholders Even when foreign exchange risks are hedged,the funding of variation margin payments on exchange traded futures can create financial distress.A well communicated hedging strategy allows debt providers to make informed decisions about Boullain Co's ability to service its debt. Agency costs and the risk of financial distress also impact the expected wealth of employees who, unlike shareholders,may not enjoy the risk reduction benefits of a diversified portfolio.A consistent hedging policy reduces the risks faced by employees which may serve to benefit Boullain Co in the form of motivational and productivity improvements. Customers and suppliers have claims on a company which create shareholder value but are conditional upon Boullain Co's survival.Suppliers may invest in production systems which create value in the form of lower costs.For customers,these claims reflect promises of quality and after- sales service levels which enable Boullain Co to charge higher prices.In both cases,shareholder value is created as long as the customers and suppliers believe these claims will be honoured.One way of achieving this is by implementing a hedging strategy and communicating it to stakeholders. In conclusion,management should attempt to communicate the principles underlying its hedging strategy and the benefits to shareholder value in the form of reduced agency and distress costs.In this way,stakeholders can make informed decisions about the potential risks and impact on their expected wealth. 答案解析： 对冲政策的基本原理 在 MM 的理论内，Boullain Co的首席执行官说公司的对冲政策无关紧要是正确的。在一个 没有交易或代理成本、市场高效且信息对称的世界里，如果股东多元化，对冲政策就没有价 值。如果与对冲相关的成本超过收益，股东价值甚至可能被破坏。 然而，现实世界中存在市场缺陷，包括破产和其他类型的财务困境。对冲通过避免潜在的毁 灭性外汇波动来保护股东价值。 主动对冲还可以通过降低债务的代理成本使债务持有人受益。明确对冲政策对股东和债务持 有人是一个比较好的信号。从这个意义上讲，对冲允许更高的杠杆率和更低的债务成本，并 减少了对限制性契约的需求。 与利益相关方沟通 即使在对冲外汇风险的情况下，为交易期货的保证金提供资金也可能造成财务困境。一个沟 通良好的对冲策略允许债权人就 Boullain Co 的偿债能力做出有利于公司的决定。 代理成本和财务困境风险也会影响员工的预期财富，与股东不同，他们可能无法享受多元化 投资组合带来的风险降低收益。对冲政策降低了员工面临的风险，这可能有助于Boullain Co 以激励和生产力提高的形式受益。 客户和供应商对一家公司要求权利是可以创造股东价值的，但要以 Boullain Co生存为前提。 供应商可以投资生产系统，以降低成本的形式给股东创造价值。对于客户来说，这些权利反 映了对质量和售后服务水平的承诺，这使得 Boullain Co 能够收取更高的价格。在这两种情 况下，只要客户和供应商相信这些权利将得到兑现，股东价值就会被创造出来。实现这一目 标的一种方法就是实施对冲策略并将其传达给利益相关方。 总之，管理层应尝试以降低代理成本和困境成本的形式传达其对冲策略的基本原则和股东价 值的好处。通过这种方式，利益相关方可以就潜在风险及其对其预期财富的影响做出明智的 决 定 。 (b)Recommend a hedging strategy for Boullain Co's foreign currency receipt in six months'time based on the hedging choices the finance director is considering.Support your recommendation with appropriate discussion and relevant calculations.(11
