stem_zh: Q2 Robson Co is a food manufacturer with a portfolio of well-known brands.The founding directors retain a significant minority shareholding in the company and continue to serve on the board following a successful listing ten years ago.After obtaining the listing,Robson Co's gearing ratio increased significantly above the sector average as the result of a poorly timed expansion strategy, mainly financed by debt.Earnings became increasingly volatile and the debt burden triggered a decline in the company's financial performance.The board responded to these problems five years ago by pursuing a debt-reduction turnaround strategy,which has been financed by a series of rights issues and asset disposals. 杠杆增加很大，收入不稳定，债务触发了公司业绩的下降。公司管理层五年前就开始采用降 低债务的方法去解决这个问题，这使得公司有了很多的配股和资产处置 Even though this strategy successfully reduced the gearing ratio,which is now equal to the industry average,the share price remains depressed due to competitive pressures within the industry.The company's credit rating has recently been downgraded once again.Robson Co's chief executive officer(CEO)has identified an opportunity to relocate the manufacturing plant and develop a state-of-the-art automated production line,which will reduce the underlying cost base and be a source of competitive advantage. 股价不好，信用评级下降， CEO 认为应该对工厂重新选址，并采用先进生产线，减少成本。 Project information Robson Co's finance director has prepared estimates of the free cash flows generated by the project,based on a four-year time horizon: The investment cost is S120m,which Robson Co's CEO proposes to finance as follows: Sm Disposal of existing manufacturing plant 20 Rights issue 10 Subsidised loan,3·5%annual interest rate 40 Bank loan,9%annual interest rate 20 Total 120 The bank loan is repayable in equal annual instalments over four years.Issue costs of 2%are payable on gross external financing and are not allowable for corporation tax.Issue costs are payable out of available cash reserves.The finance director has asked you to ignore underwriting costs relating to the rights issue. Additional information Robson Co's current equity beta is 1.418 and the debt:equity ratio is 1:5 based on market values. The risk free rate is 3%and the market risk premium is 9%.The CEO expects the business risk of the company to remain unchanged as a result of the investment. Corporation tax is payable at an annual rate of 20%. Further information on project finance The board discussed the financing of the project at a recent meeting.Robson Co's corporate bankers have already approved the funding decision for the $50m bank loan but the finance director is concerned about the following capital providers: External shareholders The last rights issue took place 18 months ago and there were two others in the previous five years.A group of shareholders have formed an action group to exert pressure on the board for more drastic change.This included a campaign to replace the CEO,which was only narrowly avoided when the shareholders voted at the most recent annual general meeting.The CEO is optimistic about the prospects of a rights issue but suggested underwriting the issue to reduce the risk of failure. 股东给了公司较大的压力，包括换掉CEO,CEO 对未来很乐观但是建议减少发行股票。 Founding directors The production and marketing directors indicated they would not be able to take up their rights due to personal financial commitments but would otherwise provide full support for the new strategy. Subsidised loan provider The government funds the subsidised loan programme to boost job creation in the economically deprived northern region of the country,which is where the new automated manufacturing plant is to be located.Although the loan has yet to be approved,the chief executive is optimistic about the outcome of their application.One feature of the loan programme is that it is open to applicants without assets available to provide security although other restrictions may be imposed.This is relevant to Robson Co since surplus assets were disposed of during the turn around strategy and those which remain willbe used to secure the new bank loan. 政府补助是为了给北方贫困区域创造就业岗位，这块儿是新的自动生产制造工业园，贷款还 没有审批下来，但是执行董事对申请的结果保持乐观。这个贷款不需要资产的抵押，但是有 其他的一些限制，因为 Robson 的一些盈余资产都被处置掉了为了战略需求，所以其他的用 来作为新贷款的抵押 (a)Calculate the adjusted present value of the investment and recommend whether the project should be accepted or not. (14marks) 问题
explanation_zh: 计算adjusted present value, 并给出投资建议 Marking Scheme (a) Base caseUngeared cost of equityIssue costsTax shield on subsidised loanTax shield on bank loanSubsidy benefitAdjusted present valueRecommendati ons14 (a) Base case Ungeared cost of equity Issue costs Tax shield on subsidised loan Tax shield on bank loan Subsidy benefit Adjusted present value Recommendati ons 14 (b)Discuss the factors the capital providers,excluding the bank,will consider before deciding whether or not to approve the funding decision for Robson Co's investment in a new manufacturing plant. (11marks) 讨论 一 下Robson 的资金提供方(除了银行)要考虑的因素，看是否要接受Robson 的 投 资 建 议。 Marking Scheme (b) Shareholders(e.g.fund availability,control,track record) Directors(e.g.signalling,asymmetries,action group) Subsidised loan provider(e.g.job creation,default risk,covenants) Max Total 4-5 25 答案解析 Project cash flows: All figures are in $m Cash flows (120·0) 20·9 20·6 28·7Discount factors-14%(w1) 1·0000·8770·7690·675Year 2 3 Cash flows (120·0) 20·9 20·6 28·7 Discount factors-14%(w1) 1·0000·8770·7690·675 Present values (120·0)18·3 15·819·4 Base case net present value =($4·6m) Base case net present value is negative and on this basis should therefore be rejected. 4 104·6 0·592 61·9 Base case net present value=($4·6m) Base case net present value is negative and on this basis should therefore be rejected Financing side effects: All figures are in $m Issue costs(w2) (2 ·0) Tax shield on subsidised loan (w3) Tax shield on bank loan(w4) Subsidy benefit(w5) Total benefit of financing side effects 2·00·9 2·0 5·7 6·6 Recommendation The adjusted present value of the project is $2·0m and so the project should be accepted. Workings: Working 1(w1):Ungeared cost of equity Beta a=be x[MVe/(MVe+MVd(1-t)) ] Beta a=1 ·418×[5/(5+1(0·8))]=1 ·222 Keu=rf+ba(rm-rf)=0·03+(1 ·222×0·09)=14% Working 2(w2):Issue costs $100mx0·02=$2,000,000 (Note:Issue costs are payable out of cash reserves,so the finance does not need to be grossed up) Working 3(w3):Tax shield on subsidised loan Annuity factor (9%,4 years)=3·240 $40mx0·035×0·20×3·240=$907,200 (Note:The risk free rate would also be acceptable as a discount rate) 算 出 税 盾， 即 节 税 金 额 Working 4(w4):Tax shield on bank loan Annual repayment=$50m/3·240=$15,432,098 Year Opening balance Interest at 9% Repayment Closing balance Tax relief on interest(20%) Discount factor(9%) Present value 1 $000 50,000 4,500 (15,432) 39,068 900 0·917 825 2 $000 39,068 3,516 (15,432) 27,152 703 0·842 592 Total present value =$1,976,000 Total present value=$1,976,000 Working 5(w5):Subsidy benefit Subsidy benefit=$40mx(0·09-0·035)×0·80×3·240=$5,702,400 3 $000 27,152 2,444 (15,432) 14,164 489 0·772 378 4 $000 14,164 1,275 (15,432) 7 255 0·708 181 DELIVERY-PLATINUM (b)Factors each capital provider may consider External shareholders The chief executive's optimism regarding the rights issue may be misplaced.Robson Co's shareholders may question the need for another rights issue so soon after the last one.Nor may they have the funds available to take up their rights,particularly when there has been a series of fund raising exercises in the last six years.Whilst in theory the shareholders are able to sell their rights,this would mean accepting a dilution in their voting power,which may not be acceptable. 配股会导致权利的进一步稀释，股东可能不会接受。 Therefore it is possible a rights issue could fail.Even if Robson Co has the issue underwritten, failure of the rights issue would have an adverse impact on Robson Co's share price and the market's confidence in the board. 因此配股可能会失败，即使R公司的配股发行是有买保险的，但是配股发行失败仍然会对公 司的股价产生负面的影响，和影响市场的信心。 Shareholders may question the logic behind the new project and whether the forecast results can be delivered.They may need reassurance that lessons from the past have been learnt.The underwriting costs have been ignored in the financial appraisal even though these are likely to be significant and may prove fatal to the final outcome,particularly when the project’s APV is quite marginal at $2m. 股东也许会问新项目背后的逻辑是否预测的结果会发生。他们可能需要一些保证的回答，保 险费在财务评价的时候被忽略了，即使他们可能价值很大并造成致命的影响特别是项目的 .APV只有$2m . The loan will mean that Robson Co's gearing once again exceeds the average and shareholders will require higher returns to compensate for the increase in financial risk.The shareholders may question whether the commitment to service and repay the new loans may mean that Robson Co will have difficulty paying an acceptable level of dividend. Founding directors The directors may also lack the funds required to take up their rights but their participation is likely to be critical to the success of the rights issue.Any reluctance to participate could send a signal to external investors that the risks involved in the new project are too high,particularly when there are information asymmetries about the project. 管理层也许也会缺少买配股的资金，但是他们的参与很重要。他们的迟疑不参与可能会对外 部的投资者传递出一个不好的信号，比如项目风险过高 Tensions between the board and shareholders have increased to the extent that the shareholder action group only narrowly lost a recent vote to replace the CEO.If the directors do not take up all of their rights and the rights issue is still successful,the balance of power may alter in favour of the action group if there was ever a renewed effort to remove the board. 董事会和股东之间紧张的关系已经增加到股东团队在最近的一次更换首席执行官的投票中 仅以微弱优势落败，如果管理层再不动用他们全部的权力或者配股仍然成功，那么股东团队 可能会进一步努力替换掉管理层。 Subsidised loan provider The subsidised loan programme provides capital for investment with the objective of boosting employment in a deprived part of the country.Since the funds ultimately originate from the taxpayer,the government is accountable for any funding decisions made.Robson Co's ability to service and ultimately repay the debt is therefore paramount.Robson Co's credit rating provides an assessment of the probability of default and the recent downgrade may cause concern.Even though Robson Co is unable to provide assets for security,the directors may still be faced with other covenants,for example,restrictions on dividends or further borrowing which may upset shareholders. The subsidy means demand for such loans is likely to be high and the selection criteria difficult, so it is unlikely that the outcome is a foregone conclusion in the way Robson Co's CEO suggests. Based on the information provided,it is unclear whether the new project would meet those selection criteria.Although Robson Co's new project is to be located in an area targeted for regeneration,it remains the case that the objective of the move is to automate the production line.Whilst jobs may still be created in a deprived area,net job creation nationwide is still likely to be negative.Whether such a policy would be attractive to the government,or the taxpayer, remains to be seen. 贷款意味着对资金的需求很高，因此 CEO 的预测是不太可能发生的。而且项目还是在贫困 的地方建造的，所以可能不太能够创造出当地的就业机会。 (Note:Credit will be given for alternative and valid comments)

stem_zh: Q3 Gogarth Co is an electrical equipment manufacturer,based in Malaysia,looking to develop its operations abroad.One of its biggest sales markets is the USA and Gogarth Co also imports components from the USA.Gogarth Co regularly hedges transactions in foreign currencies.It is currently 1 May.On 31August,Gogarth Co is due to pay$14,500,000 to an American supplier and receive $37,400000 from an American customer. 现在是五月 一 日，G 公司八月31日要支付$14,500,000美元，收到$37,400,000美元，所以时 间是从现在开始四个月到期的远期合同。 The following quotations have been obtained: Exchange rates(quoted as US dollar per Malaysian Ringgit US$/MR1) Spot Four months forward 0-2355-0-2358 0-2370-0-2374 Currency futures(contract size MR500,000,futures price quoted as USS/MR1) Futures price September 0-2378June 0-2366 September 0-2378 Currency options(contract size MR500,000,exercise price quoted as US$/MR1,premium: US cents/MR1) Exercise 0-2368 price June 0·11 Calls 0-14September 0-14 Puts June September0-19 0-23 June September Futures and options contracts mature at the month end.The number of contracts to be used should be rounded to the nearest whole number in calculations.If the amount cannot be hedged using an exact number of futures or options contracts,the amount unhedged or over-hedged should be hedged using the forward market.For the purposes of the calculations,it should be assumed that the options are exercised. 没有对冲的货币用forward market来 做 。 The head of Gogarth Co's treasury function gave a presentation about the treasury function and what it does to manage foreign exchange risk at the last board meeting.Directors have subsequently raised two questions: -The marketing director has asked whether Gogarth Co should consider using over-the-counter currency options to hedge exchange rate risk. 怎么用OTC 金融工具对冲汇率风险 --A new non-executive director has stated that he understands what the treasury function does in relation to the management of transaction risk,but is unclear on the treasury function's role in the management of economic risk. 司库部门的作用是什么 (a)Advise Gogarth Co on,and recommend,an appropriate hedging strategy for its USS cash flows on 31 August.Include relevant calculations.. (15marks) (b)Discuss the advantages and disadvantages for Gogarth Co in using exchange-traded currency options compared with using over-the-counter currency options. (5marks) (c)Discuss the role of Gogarth Co's treasury function in relation to the management of economic risk. (5marks) Requirements
explanation_zh: Marking Scheme (a) Netting of receipt and payment Forward contract Futures Buy September futures/options and number of contracts Lock in rate Expected receipt Receipt from over/under hedge Options Number of contracts Premium Receipt from over/under hedge Outcome Discussion Max 1 工 1 NN N N 15 (a)Net receipt $37,400,000-$14,500,000=$22,900,000 Forward contract $22,900,000/0·2374=MR96,461,668 Futures Buy MR September futures Basis Assume that basis reduces to zero at contract maturity in a linear fashion. Predicted futures rate =0·2366+([0·2378-0·2366]×2/3)=0·2374 六月底开始，7月底，8月底， 一共两个月的时间 Alternatively,use spot rate,0·2358+([0·2378-0·2358]×4/5)=0·2374 或者用spot rate算 Expected receipt=$22,900,000/0·2374=MR96,461,668 Number of contracts=MR96,461,668/MR500,000=192·9,say 193 Amount over-hedged=(500,000×193x$0·2374)-$22,900,000=$9,100 Payment at forward rate =$9,100/0·2370=MR38,397 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Outcome Futures(500,000x193) Payment on forward market 96,500,000(38,397)MR 96,500,000 (38,397) 96,461,603 Options Purchase MR September call options Receipt =$22,900,000/0·2368=MR96,706,081 Number of contracts =MR96,706,081/MR500,000 =193·4 contracts,approximately 193 contracts Premium=193×$0·0014×500,000=$135,100 Premium in MR,translated at spot rate=$135,100/0·2355=MR573,673 Amount under-hedged=$22,900,000-(193×500,000×$0·2368)=$48,800 Translated at forward rate =$48,800/0·2374=MR205,560 Outcome,assuming options are exercised MR Options(500,000×193) Receipt on forward market Premium 96,500,000 205,560 (573,673) 96,131,887 Recommendations The forward contract gives a marginally higher receipt than the futures.Futures would be subject to basis risk,the risk that the difference between the futures price and spot rate does not decrease linearly towards the maturity of futures.This means that the receipt may be uncertain. Futures also require a margin payment,an initial payment of cash into a margin account operated by the futures exchange,with further payments if losses are made on contracts.Options give a lower receipt,because of the need to pay a premium.Gogarth Co may consider options if it considers there is a chance that the dollar will be in a stronger position against the Malaysian ringgit than suggested by the forward rate,or if one or other transaction is likely to fall through. Forward 合同收到的前更多， future 应对的是基本风险，如果即期汇率和期货汇率没有线性 关系，那么最后收到的钱就不确定了。而期权收到的钱少，但是如果考虑到未来美元非常强 势的话，那么看涨期权可能会结算的时候收的更多 Overall,Gogarth Co should choose the forward contract as it offers the marginally higher receipt and is not subject to basis risk. Note:Other valid recommendations could be made. (b) Advantages of exchange-traded options (b)Advantages of exchange-traded options Disadvantages of exchange-traded options The price and contract details of exchange-traded options are readily available and do not need to be negotiated by Gogarth Co,unlike over-the-counter options. 标化合同，不需要额外的协商 Gogarth Co can sell exchange-traded options at any time before expiry.It may not be possible to sellover-the-counter options,although they can be allowed to lapse if not required. Exchange-traded options are less risky for Gogarth Co than over-the-counter options,due to their being subject to transparency and other regulations set by the exchange.The exchange also requires participants to set up an initial margin,so that counterparty risk should not be a problem,although margin requirements will impact Gogarth Co's cash flow. 场内交易相对来说风险小一些，规则透明，需要提前存一些保证金，所以交易的对方违约的 风险较小 Exchange-traded options are generally American style options,which means that they can be exercised at any time before their expiry date.This may be useful for Gogarth Co if transaction settlement dates are uncertain.Over-the-counter options tend to be European style options, which means that they can only be exercised on their expiry. 场内交易更多的是美式期权，因此可以随时行权结算。 Disadvantages of exchange-traded options The contract sizes and maturity date of exchange-traded options are fixed,whereas those of over-the-counter options can be negotiated to meet Gogarth Co's risk exposure.The standardisation of exchange-traded options also means that they are subject to hedge inefficiencies if the exact amount being hedged is not matched by the option contracts. 合同的对冲数量有限，标化的合同可能会因为对冲的数量与实际不相符而导致对冲低效 Over-the-counter options have a greater range of currencies available than exchange-traded options.This may be useful for Gogarth Co if it is looking to expand significantly abroad. Exchange-traded options tend to be for shorter terms than those available for over-the-counter options.Longer-term options may be better for Gogarth Co depending on transaction settlement arrangements. (c) (c) Understanding of economic risk Up to 2 marks for each well-developed point (Points could include identification of cash flows on exchange rate,role in implementing economic risk economic risk management) affected,identification management,role in of influences advising on Max Total 1 5 25 Economic risk is the longer-term risk that the present value of future cash flows may be increased or reduced by exchange rate movements.The treasury function will be involved in the development of longer-term responses,as the derivatives the treasury function will use for hedging of short-term exchange risk willnot be appropriate. 经济危机是长期的危机，司库的作用是应对长期的风险 Risk analysis The treasury function needs to identify the cash flows which may be affected by exchange rate movements.These may not just include transactions with overseas customers and suppliers. Home market sales can also be affected if,for example,the currency of the country where a foreign competitor is based weakens against the ringgit and the competitor can then afford to charge cheaper ringgit prices. The treasury function must also identify the factors affecting exchange rate movements in the longer term and assess what their impact is likely to be.This could include predicted movements, for example,changes in the economic cycle,and also the impact of sudden economic shocks.The treasury function will need to assess the impact of these exchange rate movements on Gogarth Co.This will include consideration of the other impacts which the factors affecting exchange rates will have,for example,a change in interest rate policy affecting demand for electrical equipment directly as wellas influencing exchange rate levels. 司库不仅要考虑受汇率影响的外币现金流，还要考虑本位币的现金流，因为本国市场也可能 会有外国竞争者，他们会降低我们的市场竞争力 Risk management The treasury function willbe particularly involved in determining funding policy in the context of the need to manage economic risk.One aspect of economic risk management is matching any assets held in a foreign country with a loan in that country's currency.The treasury function will determine the suitability of borrowing abroad and the best possible arrangement if foreign currency loans are required. 司库要考虑是国外借债的可行性和如果需要外币的话，最好的外币借款协议的考量。 Economic risk can also be managed by diversifying customer,supplier and operational bases and changing pricing policy.The treasury function will be involved in assessing the possible impacts of policy changes.However,the decisions will be taken in the context of operational considerations, such as supplier management,and wider strategic considerations,such as scope of operations. 经济风险可以通过把顾客，供应商，和其他日常经营的活动多样话来降低。司库要考虑政策 变化的可能的影响。这些决策对供应商管理，日常经营，战略指定方面的影响都要被考虑到 Advanced Financial Management AFM September/December 2021 AFM SeptemberDecember 2021 exam(2122 sylabus) Exam Summary Time allowed:This exam is no timed. explanation_zh: Zhichi 公司是一家大型上市工程公司，致力于为全球企业开发和制造环保产品。直到几年 前，其股票的价值一直在稳步增长，并且经常跑赢主要竞争对手。然而最近，其股票表现 不佳，许多金融分析师建议出售 Zhichi 公司的股票。 Zhichi 公司的投资者越来越担心。 分析得出的结论是，这一表现不佳是由于公司财务战略中的三项政策失误造成的，具体如 下 ： Zhichi 公司没有对其投资项目进行竣工后审计； - 几 年 来 ，Zhichi公司一直使用10%的固定折现率来评估所有投资项目。公司的高级管理 人员都不记得为什么选择这个比率；以及 Zhichi公司一直在利用股权融资为新的投资项目提供资金，分析认为这种融资策略向投 资者发出了错误的信号。 Y-PLATINUM2 New project discount rate 冈 Y-PLATINUM 101 一 | +Aomaloc Zoom Zhichi Co is considering a new projoct to manufacture ervironmentaly friondly motor scooters which are fully carbon neutral.This is a divorsification into a ncw business area for Zhichi Co in which it has no previous expenience.Zhichi Co's chief financial officor is of the opinion that Zhichi Co should determine an appropriate discount rate for the projoct based on an initial assumption that the project will be all-cquty financed. Liyu Co would be a compatitor to Zhichi Co as it manufacturos environmontally friondly motor Scootors,as wel as equipment for wind farms.Approximatoly 60%of Liyu Co's businoss is manufacturing motorscooters and the remaining 40%is manufacturing wind farm equipment. Ghon bolow is the most rocent frnancial information relating to Lyu Ca Share capital (50-25 nominal value) S20,000,000 Roserves $27,436,000 Markot value of equity $172,000,000 Market value of debt $48,260,000 Equity beta 120 Sarwenyu Co is involvod in the manufacture of equipmont for wind farms.It has estimated its cost of equity as 15-4%,andit is financed 20%by debt and 80%by equity in market value terms. The ostimated risk-froe rate is 4-8%and the market risk premium is 8%.The corporation tax rate applicable to all the companies is 20%. 题干解析： Zhichi 公司正在考虑一个新项目，生产完全碳中和的环保摩托车。这是Zhichi 公司目前的 业务领域的一个分支，并且之前没有相关经验。 Zhichi公 司 的CFO 认 为Zhichi公司应确定 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 一个合适的项目折现率，基于初始假设项目将全部为权益融资。 Liyu 公司将是Zhichi公司的竞争对手，生产环保摩托车和风力发电场设备。Liyu 公司约 60%的业务是制造摩托车，其余40%是制造风力发电场设备。 以下是 Liyu 公司最新财务信息： Share capital($0-25 nominal value) Reserves Market value of equity Market value of debt Equity beta S20,000,000 $27,436,000 $172,000,000 $48,260,000 1 ·20 Sanwenyu 正参与风力发电场设备的制造，预估的权益资本成本为15.4%;并且其20%的资 金来源于债务，80%来源于权益(按市场价值衡量)。 B预计的无风险率为4.8%,市场风险溢价为8%,公司所得税税率为20%。 B 3.New prject cash fiows -|+tonade Zoom · Zhichi Co expects the new project manufacturing envronmentally triendly motor scooters tol last for four years.The project will require an immediate expenditure of s70m for plant and machinery After the project ends in four years'time,it is anticipated that the project will be sold for S42m,inclusive of any inflationary increase. The following estimates of revenues and costs,relating directly to the project,have been made -in the first year,salos revenue is expected to be $10m and then increase to s40m in year two.In the inai two years of the project,sales revenue will grow by 20%each year Costs are estimated at 120%of salos revenue for the first year and 80%of sales revenue for the second year before reducing to 40%of sales revenue for each of the fnal two years. Substantial initial working capital of S10m wil be required at the start of year one of the project Subsequently working capital of 15%of sales revenue for that yoar will be required at the start of years two to four Any remaining working capital wll be released at the end ot the project Zhichi Co pays corporalion tax of 20%every year Tax is payable with a year's time delay and any tax losses from the project are set against the companys profits from other projects.Tax alowable depreciation is avallable on the expenditure on the plant and machinery for the project at an annual rate of 15%on a reducing balance basis.it is anticipated that ihe plant and machinery wil have a realisable value of S20m at the end of the project,and this realisable value is included in the projoct's estimated sale price of S42m. 题干解析： Zhichi 公司预计制造环保型摩托车的新项目将持续四年，该项目将需要立即支出$70m 用 于厂房和机器设备。在4年项目结束后，预计该项目将以$42m 的价格出售，包括通货膨 胀。 与项目直接相关的收入和成本估算如下： 第一年，销售收入预计为$10m, 第二年将增长至$40m。在项目的最后两年，销售收 入每年增加20%。 第一年的成本预计为销售收入的120%,第二年的成本预计为销售收入的80%,最后两 年的成本降低到销售收入的40%。 项目第一年开始时，需要$10m 的大量初始营运资本。之后，在第二年到第四年需要的营 运资本都是当年销售收入的15%,在年初需要备齐。任何剩余的营运资本营运将在项目结 束时收回。 Zhichi 公司每年缴纳20%的公司税，税款延迟一年支付，项目产生的tax loss 能用于抵扣公 司其他项目的利润。项目厂房和机器设备的 tax allowable depreciation 按15%余额递减法计 算。预计项目结束时，厂房和机器设备的可变现价值为$20m, 该可变现价值包含在项目预 计的$42m 售价中。 4.Financing the new project Due to the positive environmental nature of the new project,Zhichi Co can obtain the entire funding for the project through a loan at a subsidised interest rate of 180 basis points lower than the estimated risk-free rate of 48%.Zhichi Co's normal borrowing rate is 6%.Zhichi Co has decided that the project should be entirely funded through the subsidised loan. Issue costs,which need to be paid,are anticipated to be 3%of the gross finance.Issue costs are not allowable as a tax-deductible expense. Given that the new project is to be funded by the subsidised loan,Zhichi Co's chief financial officer is of the opinion that the adjusted present value of the project would be more appropriate than the conventional net present value,based on a risk-adjusted cost of capital,to evaluate the project.However,he cannot explain why this should be the case. 题干解析： 由于新项目的环保性质，Zhichi 公司可以通过贷款获得项目的全部资金，贷款的补贴利率 比预计的4.8%的无风险利率要低180个基点，而 Zhichi公司的正常贷款利率为6%。 Zhichi 公司决定，该项目应完全通过补贴贷款取得资金。 发行成本(需要支付)预计将占资金总额的3%,发行成本不允许税前抵扣。 鉴于新项目将由补贴贷款提供资金， Zhichi公 司CFO 认 为 ，APV 比基于风险调整的资本成 本计算出来的传统NPV 更适合评估该项目，但是他无法解释为什么是这样。 5.Future project funding 5.Future project funding □ 食| 食 Page: 一| 一 +Automatic Zoom: Zhichi Co's board of directors(BoD)is considering whether to raise funds for forthcoming projects either through new debtfinance or through asset securitisation. In adition to its manufacturing business,Zhichi Co recelves rental income on some of its factory premises and its plant and machinery which it hires to other companies.The BoD is of the opinion that it should securitise this rental income. 题干解析： Zhichi董事会(BoD) 正在考虑是否通过新的债务融资或资产证券化为即将到来的项目筹 集资金。 除制造业务外，Zhichi 公司还从其部分厂房、厂房和机器中获得租金收入，并将其租赁给 其他公司。董事会认为应将该租金收入证券化。 (a)Discuss and justify the actions Zhichi Co should take to address the three financial strategy policy failures. (8marks) Requirements解析： 要求讨论Zhichi为解决三项财务战略失败而应采取的行动，并做出解释。 Marking Scheme (a) 2-3 marks per policy failure Max Marks 8 (For example,post-completion audits:compare to budget,useful for future budget development, can be expensive and needs sufficient resources,all projects can be unique and different so not universal.Fixed discount rate:need project specific discount rate to take account of project risk, otherwise low-risk projects can be rejected and high-risk projects might be accepted.Using equity finance.Not observed in practice.Instead pecking order of internal,then debt,then equity sources of finance.Due to information asymmetry,signalling and tax advantage of debt) 答案： (a)Post-completion audits A post-completion audit is a part of monitoring and appraising capital investment projects.Its aims are to compare income, costs and timing of projects against the original budget and any subsequent changes to the budget as more information becomes known.It can be a useful learning tool to help Zhichi Co manage projects more effectively and efficiently in the future. This can lead to setting realistic budget targets,better control of costs and reduction of bottlenecks to help complete projects on time.This is especially so for similar projects.It can be an expensive process and its usefulness may be limited to projects which are unique.In order to maximise the effectiveness of post-completion audits,Zhichi Co would need to ensure that sufficient resources are provided to undertake the audit and the aims of the audit are clear and achievable. Fixed discount rate A fixed discount rate to appraise new investment projects,which Zhichi Co uses,can be ineffective when a decision is being made whether or not to undertake the project.This is because projects will have different risks attached to them and therefore the returns required from these projects would difer.This could result in low-risk projects being rejected which could have added to Zhichi Co's corporate value,and high-risk projects being accepted which could reduce corporate value.Zhichi Co should instead estimate an appropriate discount (or hurdle)rate which accounts for the risk of the project.In this way,the company can assess the value of projects more accurately and thereby add to its corporate value. New project finance Observations of how companies raise new finance for projects show that they typically prefer internal funds before accessing external markets.If new finance is raised through external markets,then companies prefer debt to equity.Debt finance may have advantages such as tax benefits and controlling the actions of managers.Investors have less information compared to managers and directors in a company,and use companies'actions on raising finance as a signal.Issuing debt finance can also be seen as a sign of confidence that the company can fufil its interest payment commitments,and can therefore be considered to be stable and less risky by investors.Information asymmetry between investors and a company's managers sends signals that the company is only raising equity finance when share prices have peaked or shares are over-valued.This causes share prices to fall ollowing announcements that a company is raising new equity finance.It is likely that Zhichi Co has experienced this.Therefore,Zhichi Co should finance through a long-term strategy of internal finance,fllowed by debt isues and then equity issues as a last resort. 答案解析： 竣工后审计 竣工后审计是监测和评估资本投资项目的一部分。其目的是将项目的收入，成本和时间与 原始预算和后续预算更改相比较。它可以成为一个有用的工具来帮助 Zhichi公司在未来更 有效地管理项目。 这会让公司制定现实的预算目标，更好地控制成本，减少瓶颈来帮助项目及时完成，特别 是对类似项目来说。这可能是一个费时费力的过程，当项目非常特殊时可能就不是非常有 用了。为了最大限度地提高竣工后审计的有效性，Zhichi公司需要确保为实施审计提供了 足够的资源，并且审计的目标是明确和可实现的。 固定折现率 Zhichi 公司使用一个固定的折现率来评估新投资项目可能是无效的，当以此做决策是否要 做该项目时。这是因为不同的项目将面临不同的风险，因此这些项目所需的回报将有所不 同。这可能导致低风险的项目(可能会增加公司价值)被拒绝，而高风险的项目(可能会 降低企业价值)被选择。 Zhichi公司应当估计一个能反映项目风险的适当折现率，这样公 司才能更准确地评估项目价值，从而增加公司价值。 新项目融资 对公司如何为项目筹集新资金的观察表明他们通常更喜欢内部资金。如果新的融资是通过 外部市场筹集的，那么公司更喜欢债务而不是权益。债务融资可能有一些优势，比如税收 优惠和控制管理层行为等。相比于公司的经理和董事，投资者接触到的信息更少，并利用 公司在筹集资金方面的行动作为信号。债务融资也可以被视为对公司能够履行其利息支付 承诺有信心的迹象，因此投资者认为公司稳定并且风险低。投资者和公司管理层之间的信 息不对称会传递出一个信号：公司只是在股价达到顶峰或股票被高估时才会进行权益融 资。这会导致公司在宣布其正进行新的权益融资后，股价下跌，很可能Zhichi 公司已经经 历了这件事情。所以Zhichi公司应首先通过长期战略性内部融资进行融资，其次进行债务 融资，最后再用权益融资。 (b)Prepare a report for the board of directors(BoD)of ZhichiCo which: ()Estimates an appropriate discount rate to use to determine the net present value of the new project based on allequity finance; (6marks) (i)Estimates the net present value of the new project,assuming that it is allequity financed; (9marks) (i)Estimates the adjusted present value of the new project; (7 marks) (iv)Evaluates whether the new project should be undertaken,and: ·discusses the assumptions made in the estimates above; ·discusses whether the adjusted present value method would be more appropriate than the conventional net present value method to evaluate the new project. (10 marks) Professional marks will be awarded in part(b)for the format,structure and presentation of the report. (4 marks) Requirements解析： 为 Zhichi 公司董事会准备一份报告，该报告包括以下内容： (i) 估计一个适合的折现率，用于计算全部为权益融资的新项目的NPV(6 (ii) 计算新项目的NPV, 假设其全部为权益融资(9分) ii) 估计新项目的APV(7 分 ) (iv) 评估新项目是否应当被执行，并且： · 讨论估计时应用到的假设(10分) · 讨论是否用APV 来评估新项目比用传统NPV 更加合适(10分) 报告的格式、结构和表述将在(b)部分获得专业分。(4分) Marking Scheme: 分 ) DELIVERY Y-PLATINUM (b)(i)(Appendix 1) Liyu Co,asset beta Sanwenyu Co,equity beta Sanwenyu Co,asset beta Asset beta attributable to business risk of motor scooter manufacturing Cost of equity/discount rate ii)(Appendix 2) Sales revenue Costs Tax allowable depreciation Tax and timing Working capital invested/released:years 0 and 1 Working capital invested/released:years 2-4 Cash flows and all-equity financed NPV (ii)(Appendix 3) Issue costs Present value of tax shield Present value of loan subsidy benefit and cost Adjusted present value (iv)Report Evaluation Discussion of assumptions (For example,rival companies'asset betas represent business risk,all-equity financed project from asset beta,variable known with certainty and will not change,interest rates will not change,debt capacity is sufficient,cost of debt represents appropriate discount rate) Discussion of appropriateness of APV (For example,limitations of cost of capital re changes in business and financial risk,APV takes account of both risks,APV separates out areas where value is derived from,APV takes into account different levels of risk and required return,but APV does not consider financial distress,tax exhaustion or agency issues) 1 2 1 1 - 1 2 1 1 2 1 1 - 1 2 1 6 2 9 2 3 7 4-52-3 4-5 4-5 Max 10 JE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Professional marks for part(b) Report format Structure and presentation of the report 1 3 答案 ： (b)Report to the board of directors(BoD),Zhichi Co Introduction This report evaluates whether or not the new motor scooter project should be undertaken.It discusses the assumptions made in estimating the value of the project and whether the adjusted present value (APV)method,which is used to estimate the value of the project,is more appropriate than the conventional net present value(NPV)method. Evaluation Although the NPV based on an all-equity financed discount rate is negative $0·9m(appendix 2),when the impact of the financing side effects is taken into account,the APV is positive $4-9m(appendix 3).On that basis,the project should be accepted if the project is funded using the subsidised loan,but not necessarily without it. However,both values are marginal and a small change in the variables(see below)could easily mean that the project is no longer viable.In addition to this,it should be noted that a large proportion of the present value of cash inflows from the project, $51-5m(appendix 2),occur in the fourth year of the project.Projections further into the future tend to be more uncertain. Zhichi Co may benefit from undertaking sensitivity and scenario analysis to assess the impact of changes in the input variables instead relying solely on the results of appendices 2 and 3. Assumptions The assumptions made in each of the three appendices are discussed in turn. In appendix one,Liyu Co's and Sanwenyu Co's asset betas are calculated by degearing each company's equity beta to eliminate the company specific financial risk.The asset betas of both companies represent just the business risk element.It is assumed that Sanwenyu Co's asset beta represents the business risk of the wind farm business and Liyu Co's asset beta represents the business risks of both the wind farm and the environmentally friendly motor scooter businesses.From these it is assumed that the asset beta,representing a suitable proxy for the business risk of environmentally friendly motor scooters,can be computed and used to estimate the all-equity financed discount rate. In appendix two,it is assumed that all input variables are known with certainty or reasonable accuracy.It is also assumed that these variables,and the factors which determine the variables,do not change in the future.Uncertainty increases as cash flows are predicted further into the future,and the majority of the positive cash flows for the new project occur in year 4. In appendix three,it is assumed that the interest rates of the subsidised loan and the corporate tax rates remain unchanged for the period of the project.The normal borowing rate of 6%is used to determine the present value of the financing side effects, although the risk-free rate could also be used and this will give a higher APV.The debt capacity of Zhichi Co could change as a result of undertaking the project. In the computations,debt beta is assumed to be zero,although in practice,corporate debt is not free of default risk. Adjusted present value or net present value With NPV,future cash flows are discounted using Zhichi Co's average cost of capital (discount rate)since a positive NPV will ensure that the minimum return requirement of all Zhichi Co's investors is met.However,the discount rate often does not take into account(i)the changing business isk profile (since the project is a diversification)nor (ii)the changing financial risk profile (since the new project will be entirely financed by debt).With the new project,both these risks are changing and the APV method takes both changes into account. Furthermore,the APV method will provide significantly more information about the sources of value and also about the different levels of risk applicable to different cash flows.When using the average cost of capital as the discount rate to generate the NPV, it is not possible to tell where the projec's value is generated from,whether the value is from undertaking the project or from the changing capital structure.It also assumes that all cash flows have the same risk profile and should therefore be discounted at the same rate.The APV method considers the risk elements separately and considers the cash flow impact of each.It also assigns a suitable cost of capital which is relevant to each cash flow (for example,the ungeared cost of equity to base case NPV and the cost of debt to the financing side effects). APV does not normally take into account costs of financial distress,possibility of tax exhaustion and agency costs related to financing using debt.However,in Zhichi Co's case,none of these is likely to be an issue because it has only used equity financing previously and therefore the impact of the above is likely to be minimal. For these reasons,APV is the more appropriate method to use to evaluate Zhichi Co's new project. Conclusion After considering the assumptions made in the calculations and discussing why the APV is the more appropriate method,the recommendation is that the new project is undertaken because it generates a positive APV.However,sensitivity and scenario analysis should be undertaken because of the assumptions made and because the decision to accept is marginal. Sanwenyu 公司的Ke=15.4%, 根据CAPM 公式【Ke=Rf+βe*(Rm-Rf)】可计算出Sanwenyu 的βe 为1.325,进一步卸杠杆算出风力发电场设备的 βa 为1.1。 最后可列出公式098=06×[asset beta,motor scooters]+0 ·4×1-1即可推导出摩托车行业 的Ba; 然后根据CAPM公式【Kei=R+βa*(Rm-Rf】算出新项目的折现率(资本成本)。 (b)(ii) 要求计算新项目的NPV, 假设其全部为权益融资。 其中 (w1)Taxation: 题中说明该项目的亏损可抵扣其他项目的利润，所以第一年亏损 12.5站在企业整体角度看是给企业省了2.5的税，省的钱也算是赚的钱，所以在算项 目现金流的时候应该把这个2.5给加上，而题中说明当年所得税下年支付，所以2.5应 该在第二年现金流当中加上。第二年同理。第三第四年产生利润要交税，递延到第四 第五年缴纳。 (w2)Working capital 按题目要求计算即可。 算出项目现金流后，由于这里假设其全部为权益融资，所以折现时按照折现率 Kei 12% (上问算出)折现。算出的NPV$(0 ·9)m为 Base case NPV。 (b)(iii) 估计新项目的APV。 APV的计算是在base case NPV (上问算出)的基础上加上与融资相关的现金流的现值 (一般就包括三项：Issue costs;Tax shield 和Subsidised loan benefit and cost)。 其中Issue costs: 项目共需要80m 初始投资(70m 买设备，10m 为working capital), 且 题中说明发行成本(需要支付)预计将占资金总额的3%。可以假设募集资金总额为X, 则 X*(1-3%)=80m,issue cost=80m/97%*3%=2·5m。 Tax shield: 根据题意，Zhichi可以按照低于无风险利率4.8%180基点的利率借款，即实际 借款利率为3%(4.8%-1.8%),而实际借款为80m, 所以每年产生利息80m×0 ·03,利 息 可以抵税，所以每年带来利息抵税80m×0.03×20%=0-5m 。 由于所得税是延后一年 支付，所以0.5m 可以看成是第2-5年的正的现金流，现值为0-5m×(4-212-0943) =1·6m; 其中4.212为折现率6%的5年年金现值系数(折现率用公司正常借款利率6% 或无风险利率4.8%都可以，答案这里用的是6%) Subsidised loan benefit and cost 算的是补贴贷款省的利息和损失的利息抵税。 Present value of subsidised loan benefit =$80m×003×3·465=$8·3m Present value of tax shield lost =$80m×0·03×02×(4·212-0·943)=$16m 最后可以得出 APV=$(0·9)m(Base case NPV)+$(2·5)m+$16m+$8·3m+$(16)m = $4-9m (6marks)(c)Compare and contrast whether Zhichi Co should raise future funding through new debt finance or through asset securitisation. (6marks) Requirements解析： 比较和对比Zhichi公司融资是应当通过新的债务融资还是通过资产证券化。 Marking Scheme: (c) Up to 2 marks per relevant point Max 6 (Maximum 3 marks if only either debt finance or asset securitisation discussed) (For example,similarity of interest and capital repayment,conventional debt imposes restrictive covenants,security provided on assets,securitisation's security provided by pooling income, creaing assets of this pool and creating different risk levels of securities,securitisation is expensive and not all income is pooled,but it gives more flexibility and does not impact debt capacity so it provides flexibility to the company) 答 案 ： (c) There are many similarities between debt finance and asset securitisation,and Zhichi Co would face similar obligations which it would need to fulfil.Conventional debt finance would raise an initial borrowed amount,on which Zhichi Co will pay interest and make capital repayments as required.When using asset securitisation,the borrowing aspect for Zhichi Co is similar in terms of repayments of interest and capital.The main differences are the bases on which the borrowing takes place. In order to mitigate or take account of risk,lenders of conventional debt would probably impose restrictive covenants on Zhichi Co and require a charge to be placed on specific assets or a pool of Zhichi Co's assets.It is likely that Zhichi Co would find it easier to raise conventional debt either through approaching specific lenders or issuing debt securities like bonds.It is also likely that raising conventional debt would be cheaper.This is because markets for conventional debt are more readily available. Asset securitisation provides a means to capitalise future income flows into an asset,in this case Zhichi Co's rental income, and then use this asset as a means of borrowing funds.Converting the future rental income into an asset would likely be undertaken through the use of a special purpose vehicle(SPV).Zhichi Co would then manage the rent collection and use the rental income stream to pay the interest and capital over a specified time period. It is likely that obtaining funding through asset securitisation can be expensive due to search costs,management costs,legal fees and continuing administrative costs.Additionall,it is unlikely that Zhichi Co would be able to borrow funds equivalent to the fullvalue of the rental income,and if rental incomes fall,Zhichi Co would need to fund the interest and capital from alternative sources.This could open up Zhichi Co to lower credit ratings and increased risk of financial distress. The advantages of asset securitisation are that Zhichi Co's debt capacity is maintained for future projects,and it provides a way for Zhich Co of utilisingincome from non-core business areas. UItimately,the choice to Zhichi Co between debt finance and asset securitisation is that the latter probably provides greater flexibility and opportunity,but at a higher cost. (Note:Credit will be given for alternative and valid comments and approaches,where relevant,for all parts of the question) 答案解析： 债务融资和资产证券化之间有许多相似之处，Zhichi 公司将面临类似的义务需要去履行。 传统的债务融资将筹集一笔初始借款金额，由Zhichi 公司支付利息并按要求偿还资本。当 使用资产证券化时，Zhichi公司的借款方面是类似的，都是利息和本金的支付。主要区别 在于借款的基础。 为了减轻或考虑到风险，传统债务的贷款人可能会对Zhichi施加限制性条款，并要求对特 定资产或Zhichi公司的资产池进行抵押。Zhichi公司可能会发现筹集传统的债务资金更加 容易，不管是通过找到特定的借款人还是通过发行债券。并且，筹集传统债务资金可能会 更加便宜，因为传统债务市场更容易找到。 资产证券化提供了一种方式，将资产未来产生的收入进行资本化，在本例中是Zhichi 的租 金收入，然后将该资产用作借款依据。将未来租金收入转换为资产可能需要通过特设机构 SPV 完成。Zhichi公司将进行租金收取管理，并使用租金收入在指定时间段内支付利息和 本金。 由于搜索成本、管理成本、法律成本和持续管理成本等因素，通过资产证券化获得资金可 能比较昂贵。此外，Zhichi 公司不太可能借到和租金收入同等的资金，并且如果租金收入 下 降 ，Zhichi 公司需要通过其他渠道筹集利息和本金，这可能会降低 Zhichi 公司的信用评 级 ，并且增加财务困境的风险。 资产证券化的优势在于， Zhichi 公司能保持目前的债务能力，并且这为Zhichi 公司利用非 核心业务领域的收入提供了一种方式。 最 终 ，Zhichi公司在债务融资和资产证券化之间的选择，后者可能提供更大灵活性和机 会，但成本更高。 (注：如果有其他有效的意见和方法也可得分) Section B-question 1 AFM September/December 2021exam (212sylabus) Symbol Highlight Strikethrough Calculator Scratch Pad Exhibits Y-PLATINUM 题干解析： 假设今天的是11月1日， Brandon Co的财务经理预计明年1月31日将需要$36m 的 短 期贷款，为海外扩张所需非流动资产投资提供资金，贷款将于5月31日偿还。 Brandon Co可以按照央行基准利率加上40个基点进行借款。央行目前基准利率为5.7%, 在过去几年中利率相对在波动，财务总监担心央行可能会在现在到1月31日之间将基准 利率提高到6.6%,以应对经济通胀压力的预期。最近的媒体报道也对基准利率下调的可能 性 进 行 了 推 测 。 Brandon Co的财务经理希望调查smoothing 作为管理公司未来利率风险敞口的一种技术， 但出于本次交易的目的，正在考虑以下对冲方式的可能性，以尽量减少利率风险敞口： - Forward rate agreements(FRAs) - Interest rate futures Options on interest rate futures 相 关 数 据 如 下 所 示 ： FRAs The following FRA rates are available: 3-4 5·82% 3-7 5·90% 4-8 5·99% Three-month $futures,$500,000 contract size Prices are quoted in basis points at 100-annual %yield. December: 94-07 March: 93·95 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM June: 93·82 Options on three-month $futures,$500,000 contract size,option premiums are in annual %. Strike price Calls Puts December March June December March June 93 ·75 0 ·168 0 ·238 0 ·323 0 ·025 0 ·087 0 ·163 假设期货和期权合约在每个月底结算。根据每月时间间隔，假设基准在合同到期时以恒定 速度降至零；假设没有基差风险，也没有保证金要求。 (a)Explain how the functional areas of a treasury department could add value to Brandon Co's restructuring plans and for each functional area,discuss the advantages of a centralised treasury department. (7 marks) Requirements解析： 解释财政部门的职能如何为 Brandon 公司的重组计划增加价值，并就每个职能讨论集中财 政司库部门的优势。 Marking Scheme: (a) Functional areas(e.g.liquidity management,currency management,funding and corporate finance) 3-4 Advantages of centralised treasury (e.g.netting off,expertise,pooling,strategic oversight and dividend policy) 3-4 Max 7 答案： (a) There are a number of ways the functional areas of a treasury department could add value to Brandon Co's expansion plans. Liquidity management As Brandon Co expands,the increase in the volume of transactions across the group will require a greater emphasis on liquidity management,including the need to support Brandon Co's working capital requirement.A centralised treasury department could add value by netting off subsidiaries'debit and credit balances,which would reduce the number of transactions and hence transaction costs. Currency management The expansion outside the eurozone introduces foreign exchange risk into the group and the need to manage the impact of currency flows on Brandon Co's earnings and shareholder value.Effective currency management,including the use of derivatives,can have a significant impact on shareholder value for companies with overseas assets.A centralised treasury department willhave the resources to employ experts with the knowledge required to manage these risks.It would also be possible to employ techniques across the group,such as matching income earned by one subsidiary with expenditure by another subsidiary in the same currency,reducing risk and the need for external hedging methods. Funding The treasury department will play a role in sourcing the appropriate debt instruments and matching maturities to the time horizon of the investment as well as managing the relationship with lenders.A centralised treasury department could add value by pooling funding requirements across the group to achieve better rates by borrowing in bulk. Corporate finance The corporate finance function would undertake the equity issue(s)to raise part of the funds required for the overseas expansion and be involved with the strategic decision-making such as formulating a dividend policy which meets the needs of shareholders.A centralised treasury department would have the appropriate level of oversight of Brandon Co's group activities to provide operational,strategic and corporate finance advice to enhance value.It can advise on short-term investment strategies,evaluate subsidiary performance and manage Brandon Co's financial structure to minimise the cost of capital. 答案解析： 财政司 库部的职能可以通过多种方式为Brandon 公司的扩张计划增加价值。 流动性管理 随着Brandon 公司的扩张，集团交易量的增加将需要更加重视流动性管理，包括支持 Brandon 公司营运资本要求。集中财政司库部可以通过抵销子公司的借贷余额来增加价 值，这会减少交易次数，从而降低交易成本。 货币管理 欧元区以外的扩张给集团带来了外汇风险，需要管理货币流动对 Brandon 公司的收益和股 东价值的影响。有效的货币管理，包括使用衍生工具可能对拥有海外资产的公司的股东价 值产生重大影响。 一个集中的财政司库部门将有资源聘请具有风险管理知识的专家。这也 将可能在整个集团中实行相关的技术，例如将一个子公司的收入与采用同样货币的另一个 子公司的费用匹配，降低了风险和对外部对冲的需求。 资金提供 财政司库部门将能够采用适当的债务工具和匹配投资的到期日.并且能管理与债权人的关 系 。司库集 中财政部可以增加价值通过集中整个集团的资金需求.通过大量借贷来得到更 有利的利率。 公司财务 公司财务职能部门将负责股权发行以筹集海外扩展所需的部分资金，并参与战略决策，如 制定符合股东需求的股息政策。集中的司库财政部门将对 Brandon 公司的集团活动进行适 当的监督，来提供运营、战略和企业财务方面的建议，以提高价值。该部门可以为短期投 资提供建议战略、评估子公司业绩并管理 Brandon 公司的财务结构，以最大限度地降低资 本成本。 (b)Recommend a hedging strategy for the $36m loan based on the hedging choices the treasury manager is considering,if the central bank base rate increases to 6.6%.Support your answer with appropriate calculations. Note:Up to 4 marks are available for discussion. (15 marks) Requirements解析： 如果央行基准利率提高到6.6%,根据财政司库部经理正在考虑的对冲选择，为$36m 贷款 推荐一个对冲策略。通过适当的计算支持您的答案。 (注：讨论部分最多4分) Marking Scheme: (b) FRA Go short on March futures Number of contracts Basis Impact of interest rate increase with futures Buy March puts Premium calculation Exercise Impact of interest rate increase on options Discussion 答案： (b)FRAs FRA rate 5·90%(3-7)since the funds will be required in three months'time for a period of four months. If central bank rate increases to 6·6% Interest payment:(6·6%+0·4%)x 4/12x$36,000,000 Receipt from bank:(6·6%-5·9%)×4/12x×$36,000,000 Net borrowing cost Effective annual interest rate:756,000/36,000,000x12/4 2 1 2 1 1 1 2 1 1 1 Futures Hedge against an increase in interest rates,therefore go short in futures market.Use March contracts,as funds will be required on 31 January. Number of contracts =$36,000,000/$500,000×4 months/3 months =96 contracts Basis Spot price(1 November)-futures price =basis (100-5·70)-93·95=0·35 Unexpired basis on 31 January=2/5×0·35=0-14 If central bank rate increases to 6·6% $ Interest payment as above 840,000 Expected futures price:100-66-0·14=93·26 Profit on the futures market:(0·9395-0·9326)x$500,000×3/12×96 (82,800 ) Net borrowing cost 757,200 Effective annual interest rate:757,200/36,000,000x12/4 6·31% Options on interest rate futures Buy March put options to hedge against an increase in interest rates.As above,96 contracts are required. Premium=0·00087x$500,000×3/12×96=$10,440 If centra bank base rate increases to 6·6% Exercise price Expected futures price,as above Exercise? Gain in basis points Interest payment as above Profit on options ·0049x$500,000×3/12×96 Premium Net borrowing cost Effective annual interest rate:791,640/36,000,000×12/4 ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Buy put 93 ·75 93 ·26 Yes 49 $ 840,000 (58,800) 10,440 791,640 6 ·60% 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION ONUJNE DELMERY-PLATMLM Advice The forward rate agreement and futures market provide broadly similar results.The outcome of the forward rate agreement hedge is marginally lower and may be the prefered choice.In theory,both methods provide a fixed interest payment and a certain cash outflow but futures contracts are subject to basis risk and margin requirements. If the central bank rate increases to 6·6%,the option is the least attractive hedging method due to the expensive premium. Unlike forward rate agreements and futures contracts,however,options do not have to be exercised and allow Brandon Co to benefit from upside potential if the central bank base rate falls.Treasury staff would need to assess the likelihood and amount of a possible reduction in interest rates when assessing the viability of the option. If the board is to achieve its objective of minimising risk exposure,the certain cash flow associated with the forward rate agreement willbe more attractive than the variable outcome associated with the options. 答案解析： 计算部分： 1)FRA: 根据题意，现在是11月1日，将要在1月31日(3个月后)借款36m, 且在5月31日 还款(7个月后),所以选择3-7 FRA, 锁定借款利率为5.9%。 3个月后，当借款利率提高到6.6%时，企业实际借款利率为6 ·6%+0 ·4%(题中说明企业借 款利率要在基准利率基础上上浮40个基点);企业借款36m, 借4个月，需要向银行支 付(6-6%+0 ·4%)×4/12×$36,000,000=840,000的利息，而与银行签订了FRA 协议，锁 定利率为5.9%,所以银行会补贴给企业(6-6%-5.9%)×4/12×$36,000,000=$84,000。 最终企业实际借款成本为756,000,实际借款利率为756,000/36,000,000×12/4=6-3% (考试要算出年化利率) 2)Futures: 在借款情形下，现在应当卖出利率期货。(担心利率上升，而利率期货价格标价方式为 100-r, 所以在利率上升情况下，利率期货价格下降；所以利率期货的交易方向应当为先卖 后买，即short futures)。 由于要在1月31日借款，所以选择离该日期最近的March contracts 交易。 根据公式，Numbers of contacts = ,可以计算出卖出 的期货合同的数量为=$36,000,000/$500,000×4 months/3 months =96 contracts。 现在(11月1日)基差(现货价格和期货价格的差)为(100-5 · 70)-93-95=0 · 35,11月 1日到期货到期日3月31日共5个月，而我们要在1月31日平仓(离期货到期日还有2 个月；考试中假设基差均匀消灭为0,所以在1月31日，基差(Unexpired basis) 应当为 2/5×0·35=0·14。 以此可以根据公式 Expected future price=(100-Prevailing spot rate)-Unexpired Basis推 算出，到1月31日，当实际利率上涨到6.6%时，当天期货价格为100-66-0 · 14= 93·26。 所以在期货市场上，是按照0 ·9395卖，按照0 ·9326买，一共能够赚到(0 ·9395-0 ·9326) x$500,000×3/12×96=82,800。 在现货市场上，实际借款利息为(6 ·6%+04%)×4/12×$36,000,000=840,000。 综 上 ，选 用future, 实际借款成本为757,200,Effective annual interest rate= 757,200/36,000,000×12/4=6-31%(年化利率) 3)Options on interest rate futures 由于是借款，担心利率上升，而利率期货价格标价方式为100-r, 所以在利率上升情况 下，利率期货价格下降，所以要在期权上赚钱，应当买入看跌期权(未来利率期货价格下 降时，可以按照行权价卖，从而能够赚取收益);且在1月31日借款，所以选择离该日 期最近的March option 。(March put options的期权费为0.087%。 需要买入看跌期权合同和上述卖出的期货合同数量一致，为96 contracts。 所以共需要支付期权费0 ·00087×$500,000×3/12×96=$10,440。 当1月31日利率上涨到6.6%,预计的利率期货价格为100-66-0 · 14=93.26 (Future 那里算过),看跌期权行权价为93.75,所以选择行权，行权能赚到49个基点，赚到 0·0049×$500,000×3/12×96=$58,800。 所以使用Option, 实际借款成本=实际需要支付的利息840,000-期权上赚到的钱 58,800+期权费10,440=791,640。 最终 Effective annual interest rate:791,640/36,000,000×12/4=660% Advice 部分： 远期和期货提供了大致相似的结果。远期的结果更低，可能是首选。理论上，这两种方法 都提供固定利息支付和特定现金流出，但期货合约受基准基差风险和保证金要求的制约。 如果央行利率提高到6.6%,由于期权费昂贵，期权是最不具吸引力的对冲方法。然而，与 远期利率协议和期货不同，期权不必行使；如果央行基准利率下降，期权将使 Brandon 公 司受益。财政司库部门员工需要评估利率下行的可能性和金额，从而评估期权的可行性。 如果董事会要实现其最小化风险敞口的目标，将产生特定现金流的远期将比与会产生可变 结果的期权更有吸引力。 (c)Briefly explain what is meant by smoothing and how this technique could be used to manage Brandon Co's interest rate risk. (3marks) Requirements解析： 简述解释smoothing 的含义，以及该技术如何用于brandon 公司的利率风险管理。 Marking Scheme: (c) Up to 2 marks per relevant point Max 6 (Maximum 3 marks if only either debt finance or asset securitisation discussed) (For example,similarity of interest and capital repayment,conventional debt imposes restrictive covenants,security provided on assets,securitisation's security provided by pooling income, creating assets of this pool and creating different risk levels of securities,securitisation is expensive and not all income is pooled,but it gives more flexibility and does not impact debt capacity so it provides flexibility to the company) 答案： (c)Smoothing Smoothing is a relatively straightforward technique for managing interest rate risk.Under this approach,Brandon Co would make an assessment about possible interest rate movements and divide its debt between variable and fixed rate loans according to what it could afford. If the central bank increases the base rate to 6·6%,only the variable rate loans will cost more.The fixed rate will be relatively cheaper and will reduce the overall inancial burden compared to the scenario where all the loans are at a variable rate.If the base rate falls,Brandon Co benefits from a cheaper variable rate but will still have to service its fixed rate debt at the higher rate. 答案解析： Smoothing Smoothing 是利率风险管理的一种相对简单的技术。在这种方法下， Brandon 公司将对可 能的利率变动进行评估，并将其能负担的起的债务分为可变利率贷款和固定利率贷款。 如果央行将基准利率提高到6.6%,那么只有可变利率贷款的成本会更高。固定利率将相对 便宜并减轻总体财务负担，与全都是可变利率的情况相比。如果基准利率下降， Brandon 公司受益于更便宜的 的可变利率，但仍将按照一个更高的利率偿还其固定利率债务。

explanation_zh: Hanwood Shoes Co于40年前开始经营，在商店生产和销售儿童鞋。这些鞋子的质量比竞 争对手生产的鞋子高，但是也更贵。尤其是校园鞋的销售一直是现金的主要来源，有助于 为公司的扩张提供资金。 大约15年前， Hanwood Shoes Co 公司开始制造和销售成人鞋。Hanwood Shoes Co 的成 人鞋目前的销售利润率高于儿童鞋。成人鞋的销售现在创造了Hanwood Shoes Co的大部 分利润。出售的成人鞋是一种使用寿命长的正式鞋和一些时尚鞋的结合。成人鞋的库存周 转期平均明显高于儿童鞋。 Hanwood Shoes Co 由两个独立的儿童和成人鞋类部门组成。它的商店出售儿童鞋或成人 鞋，但不是两者都卖。Hanwood Shoes Co也向其他零售商销售成人鞋。 五年前，Hanwood Shoes Co 重组了其成本基地。它将儿童鞋的生产转移到本国的新工 厂，并将成人鞋的生产外包给外国供应商。然而，Hanwood Shoes Co董事会现在预测儿 童鞋的利润增长速度将低于成人鞋。该公司预计，在未来几年，儿童鞋市场的更大份额将 被成本较低的公司占据。 Hanwood Shoes Co 董事会意识到，它必须让投资者相信，它所做的任何重大战略改变都 会导致每股收益的增加。投资者会根据公司业务风险状况的变化考虑每股收益的变化。 Exhibit 2 2.Sale of chilcren's shoes division +Aonalo Zoom ERY-PLATINUM Hanwood Shoes Co's board wishes to improve the company's cash position for two reasons. First,loan notes of $175m nominal value are due to be redeemed in just over a year's time.The board is unwilling to seek renewed loan funding because the terms would not be favourable and some investors have expressed concern about gearing levels.Second,Hanwood Shoes Co has recently been slow to pay some of its suppiers of adults'shoes One major supplier recently delayed delivery of shoes to Hanwood Shoes Co until it was paid for previous deliveries. Hanwood Shoes Co's chief executive proposed that the company should in future only sell adults' shoes and should sell the children's shoes division.He believed from industry contacts that at least three children's clothing manufacturers,looking to expand their product range,would be interested.The finance director suggested that the proceeds from the sale of the division could first be used to pay off the loan notes.It would then be used to increase cash held and thus improve liquidity The remaining proceeds would be invested in the adults'shoes division to improve earnings per share. A non-executive director asked why liquidity needed to be improved.He claimed that higher profits could be achieved by investing funds not required to repay the loan notes in non-current assets.He thought that Hanwood Shoes Co should maintain the same level of current assets as it has now 高顿教育 GOLDEN EDUCATION 题干解析： Hanwood Shoes Co董事会希望改善公司的现金状况有两个原因。 首先，面值$175的债券将在一年多时间内赎回。董事会不愿意寻求新的债务融资，因为条 件不太有利，一些投资者对杠杆水平表示担忧。其次，Hanwood Shoes Co最近迟迟不向 其一些成人鞋供应商付款。一家主要供应商最近推迟了向 Hanwood shoes Co 交付鞋子的 时间，直到支付了之前的交付款项。 Hanwood Shoes Co的 CEO 提议，该公司今后应该只销售成人鞋，并出售儿童鞋部门。他 从行业调研中得出结论：至少有三家儿童服装制造商希望扩大其产品范围，他们会感兴 趣。CFO 建议，出售该部门的价款应首先用于偿还债券，其次将用于增加持有的现金从而 改善流动性。剩余款项将投资于成人鞋部门，以提高每股收益。 一位非执行董事询问为什么需要改善流动性。他声称不需要偿还债券而将资金用于固定资 产投资将可以获得更高的利润。他认为 Hanwood Shoes Co应该保持目前的流动资产水 平。 Exhibit 3 3.Impact an consequences of sale 冈 101 Adomadie Zoom · The salos price of the chikdron's shoos divsion wll be the sum of the present value of predlicted future froe cash flows.The discount rate to be used is 10%.

explanation_zh: 以上为 Hanwood shoes Co当前资产负债表。 (a)Calculate the expected sales price of the children's shoes division and demonstrate its impact on Hanwood Shoes Co's statement of financial position and forecast earnings per share. (13 marks) Requirements解析： 计算童鞋部门的预期销售价格，并阐述其对 Hanwood Shoes Co 资产负债表和预计每股收 益的影响。 Marking Scheme: (a) PV of free cash flows Years 1-4 PV of free cash flows Year 5 onwards Revised statement of financial position Revised eps 答案： (a)Proceeds from sales of children's shoes division Year Free cash flows Discount factor 10% Present value Present value 4 $m2 $m1$m3 4 $m 2 $m 1 $m $m 858876 81 85 88 0·9090·8260·7510·683 646069 67 64 60 260 Growth rate year 5 onwards=3·5% Present value in year 5 onwards =($88m×1 ·035/(0·10-0·035)×0·683=$957m Total present value =$260m+$957m =$1,217m Impact on statement of financial position($m) Profit on sale =$1,217m-$608m-$349m=$260m Current assets receipt from sale =$1,217m Current assets adjustment =($894m×1·4)-($909m-$349m)-$1,217m=($525m) Gain in current assets =$1,217m-$525m =$692m Alternative working Gain in current assets=($894m×1·4)-($909m-$349m)=$692m ONLINE DELIVERY-PLATINUM Non-current asset adjustment =$525m-$175m =$350m Original Sales Adjustments Final $m $m $m $m Assets Non-current assets 1,2171,200 (608) 350 942 Non-current assets 1,217 Current assets 909 (349) (525) 1,252 Total assets 2,109 2,194 Equity and liabilities EDU Called-up share capital 50 50 Reserves 737 260 997 Total equty 787 1,047 9%loan notesNon-current liabilities 175 (175) 0 9%loan notes 7%loan notes 145 145 Bank loans 108 108 Total non-current liablities 428 253 Current liabilities 894 894 Total equity and liabilities 2,109 2,194 PLATINUM Impact on eps($m) Predicted post tax profits Less:Profits from children's shoes Add:Interest saved,net of tax($175m×9%x(1-0·2)) Add:Return on additional non-current assets($350mx18%x(1-0·2)) Add:Return on additional current assets($692mx6%x(1-0·2)) Adjusted profits Number of shares Adjusted eps Current forecast Revised forecast 217217 217 (76) 13 50 33 21750m237 217 50m 50m $4·34$4·74 $4·34 答案解析： 1)童鞋部门的预期销售价格： 1-4年现金流按10%折现，现值为260; 题中假设第4年后，童鞋部门的自由现金流将按照每年3.5%的速度增长，所以第5年及其 以后的现金流构成永续增长现金流，永续增长现金流现值公式为PV=CF1/(i-g),所以第5 年及其以后的现金流的现金流折到4时点的价值为$88m×1035/(0·10-0035), 进一步 折现到0时点现值为$88m×1035/(0 ·10-0 ·035)×0 ·683=$957m。 综上，童鞋部门未来现金流现值共计$260m +$957m =$1,217m。 2 ) 对Hanwood Shoes Co资产负债表的影响： 根据题意：出售童鞋部门获得的价款将首先用于偿还9%的债券(175m) 。 销售处置收 入 的剩余部分将作为流动资产的一部分，从而使流动比率增加到1.4。其余的处置销售收入将 投资于非流动资产 。 首先，Profit on sale =$1,217m-$608m-$349m=$260m,年底结转至Reserve。 其次，由题意可知流动资产目标金额为$894m×14=$1252m, 所以可以假设获得的价款 投入到流动资产中的金额为 X, 则909-349(处置的童鞋部门的流动资产)+X= 1252,X=692m。 由此可算出投入到NCA 中的金额为1217-175-692=350。 综上，调整之后的NCA=1200-608 (处置的童鞋部门的NCA)+350=942, 调整之后 的流动资产=1252,调整之后的Reserves=737+260=997, 调整之后的99% loan note=0。 3 ) 对 EPS 的影响：见答案计算过程。 (b)D iscuss whether Hanwood Shoes Co's investors are likely to be satisfied with the proposed sale of the children's shoes division and its consequences for profits and funding. (7 marks) Requirements解析： 讨论Hanwood Shoes Co的投资者是否会对出售童鞋部门的提议及其对利润和资金的影响 感 到 满 意 。 Marking Scheme: Max 7(b) Up to 2 marks per relevant point Max 7 (relevant points can include need to sell,timing,amount,less diversification,alternative strategies, impact on eps impact on funding) 答案： (b)Sale Investors may query the need for the sale at this time.Although the board expects the children's shoes market to become more competitive in a few years'time,this has not yet happened.Investors may wonder if a better sales price could be obtained in a few years'time with a few more years'growth.Although some investors are concerned about gearing,a sale to reduce debt funding may seem to be a forced sale,and investors may ask whether alternative strategies could resolve the problems. Investors may also wonder why Hanwood Shoes Co is not exploring the possibility of selling the production facilities (if sale proceeds equalled net book value,that would be enough to pay off the 9%loan notes).Hanwood Shoes Co could then use the same outsourcing business model for producing children's shoes which it does for adults'shoes. Investors may also be concerned whether Hanwood Shoes Co is achieving best value from the sale if the company is going to be selling the division at $1,217m.Assuming a higher growth rate (perhaps by taking the geometric average of growth over the next four years)would give a higher selling price.Hanwood Shoes Co could also be expected to ask for a premium on the sale,given that a number of companies looking to develop their sales of children's shoes may be interested.That said,the cash flows in the calculation are taken to infinity,which is optimistic. Investors would also wonder about the decreased diversification that the sale would mean.Although adults'shoes achieve higher margins than children's shoes,they may need more investment to maintain their position,and the appeal of fashionable ranges may be uncertain.Children's school shoes have been a reliable cash generator.Ultimately also,the investors may feel that they are investing in a smaller company,given the decrease in the non-current base and range of products,and may want to invest elsewhere in a company offering better growth prospects. Profits The forecast increase in earnings per share is 9·2%,but investors will question whether the assumptions on which the forecast is based are optimistic.They will also take into account the risks of the business being less diversified as it will olely be selling adult shoes.However,part of the increase in profits is a fall in the commitment to paying interest,which investors may view positively if they are concerned about gearing. Funding The sale will help fund an improvement in the gearing of the company.The funds will not only be available to pay of the 9% loan notes immediately,they could also be used to pay offthe bank loan and/or the other loan notes in time,or reduce trade payables as they may be being used as a source of short-term funding.However,investors might also wonder how future investment in adults'shoe shops would be funded,particularly if future sales did not meet current expectations.Current lenders may have placed restrictions on Hanwood Shoes Co seeking new borrowing from other sources.If Hanwood Shoes Co was able to seek additional loan capital,it would have a smaller non-current asset base to offer as security,which may affect lenders' attitudes. 答案解析： 出售 投资者可能会质疑此时出售的必要性。尽管董事会预计童鞋市场在几年竞争会越来越大， 但这还没有发生。投资者可能会想，是否可以在几年后再增长几年能卖到一个更好的价 钱。尽管一些投资者担心杠杆率，但为了减少债务而进行出售似乎是一种强制出售，投资 者可能会问是否有其他策略可以解决这个问题。 投资者可能还想知道为什么 Hanwood Shoes Co不寻找出售生产设备的可能性(如果出售 价款等于账面净值，足以偿还9%的贷款)。 Hanwood Shoes Co 可以使用和生产成人鞋的 相同的外包模式来生产童鞋。 投资者可能还担心，如果 Hanwood Shoes Co打算按照$1217m 出售，是否能从出售中获 得最佳价值。假设更高的增长率(可能在未来四年将进行几何增长)将给出更高的售价。 Hanwood Shoes Co也可能在出售时收取溢价，考虑到许多希望发展童鞋销售的公司会感 兴趣。也就是说，现金计算中的流量取为无穷大，这是乐观的。 投资者也会对此次出售意味着的多元化程度下降感到疑惑。尽管成人鞋利润率高于童鞋， 他们可能需要更多的投资来维持自己的地位，以及时尚的吸引力范围可能不确定。童鞋一 直是可靠的现金来源。最终，投资者可能会感到他们正在投资一家较小的公司，由于到非 流动资产基础和产品范围的减少，并且想在其他地方投资一家有更好增长前景的公司。 利润 每股收益的预测增长率为9.2%,但投资者会质疑预测所依据的假设是乐观的。他们还将考 虑到业务多元化程度降低的风险，因为它将只销售成人鞋。但是，利润增加的部分原因是 支付利息的下降，投资者可能会认为这一点是积极的，如果他们关注杠杆率的话。 资金提供 此次出售将有助于提高公司的资产负债率。这些资金不仅可以用来偿还9%债券也可以用于 及时还清银行贷款和/或其他债券，或减少交易应付款项，因为它们可能被用作短期资金来 源。然而，投资者也可能想知道未来对成人鞋店的投资将如何得到资金，特别是如果未来 的销售额达不到目前的预期。当前贷款人可能对 Hanwood Shoes Co寻求其他来源的新借 款施加了限制。如果Hanwood Shoes Co能够寻求额外的债务资金，它将只有一个较小的 非流动资产作为担保基础，这可能会影响借款人的态度。 (c)Discuss the implications for future profits and liquidity of the alternative suggestion made by the nonexecutive director. Requirements解析： 讨论非执行董事提出的建议对未来利润和流动性的影响。 Marking Scheme: (c) Impact on profits Impact on liquidity Max 答案： (c)Implications for profits If the surplus after the loan repayment was solely used to fund non-current asset investment,this would,based on the assumptions about the return on non-current assets,increase earnings by $33m(($1,252m-$909m)×(18-6)%×(1- 0·2)),representing a total increase of $53m or 24·4%.This would be more acceptable to the shareholders.However,it might not be possible to sustain the 18%return on non-current assets over a larger investment,certainly in the short term.Hanwood Shoes Co might also consider the impact of the total investment occurring over a longer period of time. Implications for liquidity However,the proposed policy would not result in the one-0ff improvement in liquidity.Hanwood Shoes Co is currently having dificulty paying suppliers,suggesting its current ratio is too low at present.Going forward,the increased volume of activity together with the longer turnover time of adult shoes compared with children's shoes would probably mean an increase in inventory,and decreased liquidity,making it more dificult to pay suppliers on time.Problems paying suppliers would become more significant as Hanwood Shoes Co would be dependent in future on suppliers forall its shoes,rather than having children's shoes made in the factory. Hanwood Shoes Co might have to review its mix of working capital,particularly how much inventory it holds,in order to maintain sufficient liquidity.There might need to be a reduction in inventory holding times and a reduction in lines being stocked,particularly slow-moving lines. 答案解析： (c)Implications for profits If the surplus after the loan repayment was solely used to fund non-current asset investment,this would,based on the assumptions about the return on non-current assets,increase earnings by $33m($1,252m-$909m)x(18-6)%x(1- 0·2)),representing a total increase of $53m or 24·4%.This would be more acceptable to the shareholders.However,it might not be possible to sustain the 18%return on non-current assets over a larger investment,certainly in the short term.Hanwood Shoes Co might also consider the impact of the total investment occurring over a longer period of time. Implications for liquidity However,the proposed policy would not result in the one-off improvement in liquidity.Hanwood Shoes Co is currently having dificulty paying suppliers,suggesting its current ratio is too low at present.Going forward,the increased volume of activity together with the longer turnover time of adult shoes compared with children's shoes would probably mean an increase in inventory,and decreased liquidity,making it more difficult to pay suppliers on time.Problems paying suppliers would become more significant as Hanwood Shoes Co would be dependent in future on suppliers forall ts shoes,rather than having children's shoes made in the factory. Hanwood Shoes Co might have to review its mix of working capital,particularly how much inventory it holds,in order to maintain suficient liquidity.There might need to be a reduction in inventory holding times and a reduction in lines being stocked,particularly slow-moving lines. 对利润的影响 如果贷款偿还后的剩余资金仅用于非流动资产投资，则根据假设的非流动资产的回报率， 将收益增加$33m. 总的增长将达到$53m 或增长24.4%.这将更为股东所接受。然而.在 大型规模投资中维持18%的非流动资产回报率可能不太可能，尤其是在短期内。 Hanwood Shoes Co还需要考虑在更长时间内总投资的影响。 对流动性的影响 提议的政策不会导致流动性得到一次性改善。Hanwood Shoes Co目前难以向供应商付 款，这表明其流动比率太低。未来，成人鞋(和儿童鞋相比周转天数更长)作业量的增加 很可能导致库存上升和流动性下降，导致更难及时向供应商付款。难以向供应商及时付款 的问题更会凸显出来，因为Hanwood Shoes Co未来将依赖供应商供应所有鞋子，而不是 儿童鞋公司可以自己在工厂生产。 Hanwood Shoes Co 可能需要审查其营运资本组合，特别是其持有的库存量，以便保持足 够的流动性。他可能需要减少库存持有时间和减少库存业务线，特别是周转速度慢的业务 线。 Advanced Financial Management AFM 2022 December AFM December 202222/23 syllabus)P Flag for Review AFM December 202222/23 syllabus) P Flag for Review Section A This section of the exam contains one question. (6 marks) (b)Prepare a report for the Board of Directors(BoD)of Fondir Co which: (i)Calculates the amounts receivable in$from Lothil in four months'time,using the over-the-counter (OTC)forward rate and the OTC option; (7 marks) (ii)Calculates the interest return in $of the cash flows from Lothil when futures contracts are used to hedge the interest rate fluctuations of 0.5%.Calculations should include the number of futures contracts needed and the gain or loss in the futures market in$; (9 marks) (iii)Comments on the results obtained in(b)(i)and (ii),and addresses the queries raised by the BoD with respect to alternative methods to hedge the receipt and the margin requirements; (9 marks) (iv)Discusses if it would be beneficial for Fondir Co to manage its financial risks,and whether or not the company should communicate its risk management approach to its stakeholders. (9 marks) Professional marks will be awarded for the demonstration of skill in communication, analysis and evaluation,scepticism and commercial acumen in your answer. (50 marks) 1.Fondir Co,risk management -includes details on communication Fondir Co,based in the USA(currency $),is involved in the production and sale of high- quality foods under its popular brand called 'Delibeli'.Initially,its products were mainly aimed at the USA market,but increasingly the products are sold internationally to North and South America and to Europe.This rapid growth has meant that Fondir Co is expanding its production facilities and receiving its revenues in a variety of currencies. Fondir Co has developed robust systems to manage its operational risks,but so far it has not managed its financial risk from currency and interest rate fluctuations.Fondir Co's board of directors(BoD),on the whole,is supportive of the opinion that the company's financial risks should be managed using derivative products,and the company's approach to risk management should be communicated to its main stakeholder groups including shareholders,managers,lenders and employees.AC Fondir Co's marketing director questioned whether these and other financial risks should be managed at all.He suggested that the costs of managing such risks would outweigh the benefits derived from the management of these risks,and therefore would be disadvantageous for the company. Fondir Co's finance director(FD)argued that although many risks can be managed using derivatives,some risks were harder to manage.She gave the Italian market as an example,where direct competitors from France and Germany have successfully penetrated the Italian market.As a result,Fondir Co has found that its sales revenue from the Italian market has been falling and the FD suggests that this is due to the weakening of the Euro(€)(the Italian currency)against the$.The FD is of the opinion that this relative weakening of the€is likely to continue for some time and cannot be managed through the use of derivative instruments. 2.Receipt from Lothil Europe,whose currency is the Lothil Lira(LL).It is expecting a receipt of LL357m in four months'time,on 1 May.Although there are no exchange-traded derivative products available for hedging the Lothil Lira,Fondir Co has access to two over-the-counter (OTC) derivative products. The first is a four-month forward rate agreed today,based on the Lothil annual base rate of 6%plus 60 basis points and the USA annual base rate of 3·3%less 30 basis points.The spot exchange rate between Lothil and the USA is LL84·00/$1. The second is an OTC$call or put option with an exercise price of LL84·00.The premium for the calloption is LL4·00 per $1 and for the put option it is LL3·00 per $1.The option premium is payable at the commencement of the option contract,and Fondir Co would borrow the money needed for the premium using its overdraft facility.The company pays interest on its overdraft at an annual rate of 5·4%. The BoD wants to know how the currency exposure of this receipt can be managed using the two instruments above,and also whether it is possible to hedge this using alternative methods and not derivative instruments. 3.Short-term investment Fondir Co will choose the more financially beneficial hedging instrument to hedge the cash receipt from Lothil.Once the cash has been received in four months' time,on 1 May and exchanged into $,it will be invested for a further five months before the cash is needed elsewhere.Fondir Co's BoD wants to know how interest rate futures can be used to hedge against an increase or decrease of 0·5%in the base rate.Fondir Co can invest funds on a short-term basis at the USA base rate(which is currently 3·3%)less 30 basis points. The following information is provided for June futures: The three-month $futures contracts have a contract size of $500,000.The June futures contract price of 96-10 is quoted in terms of basis points at 100-annual %yield. In the calculations the following can be assumed: -Settlement of the futures contracts is at the end of the month; -Basis diminishes to zero at contract maturity,based on monthly time intervals; -There is no basis risk; -Margin requirements can be ignored. The BoD has heard that dealing with futures can be expensive because of margin requirements.They want to know what margins are and whether or not dealing with futures is expensive because of this. 题干文字部分解析： 1.风险管理-包括沟通的细节 总部设在美国的Fondir公司(货币是美元)从事的是高品质食品的生产和销售，旗下品牌 为“Delibeli”。最初，其产品主要针对美国市场，不过有越来越多的产品销往全球，比 如北美、南美和欧洲。这种快速增长意味着 Fondir 公司正在扩充其生产设施，并通过 多种货币获得收入。 Fondir 公司开发了强大的系统来管理其运营风险，但迄今为止，它尚未管理货币和利 率波动带来的财务风险。总的来说，Fondir 公司的董事会支持使用金融衍生品管理公 司财务风险的观点，并且公司的风险管理方法应传达给其主要利益相关群体，包括股 东、经理、债权人和员工。 Fondir 公司的营销总监质疑是否应该管理这些和其他金融风险。他认为，管理这些风 险的成本将超过管理这些风险带来的收益，因此对公司不利。 Fondir 公司的财务总监认为，虽然许多风险可以利用衍生工具来管理，但有些风险更 难管理。她以意大利市场为例，来自法国和德国的直接竞争对手已经成功打入意大利 市场。因此，Fondir 公司发现其来自意大利市场的销售收入一直在下降，财务总监表 示这是由于欧元(毛)(意大利货币)对美元的疲软。财务总监认为毛的相对疲软可能会持续 一段时间，并且无法通过使用衍生品金融工具进行管理。 2.来自Lothil 的收入 欧洲某国的货币是 Lothil L ira (LL)。Fondir 公司预计在4个月后，即5月1日，收到一 笔价值 LL 357m 的收入。虽然没有可用于对冲 LL 的交易所交易的金融衍生产品，但 Fondir公司有两种场外(OTC) 衍生产品。 第一个是今天商定的4个月远期利率，基于Lothil年化基本利率6%加60个基点，以及 美国年基本利率3.3%减30个基点。LL 和美元的即期汇率是LL84.00/$。 第二种是场外美元看涨或看跌期权，行权价为 LL 84.00。买入看涨期权的期权费是每1 美元 LL 4.00,买入看跌期权的期权费是每1美元LL 3.00。期权费应在期权合同开始时 支 付 ，Fondir 公司将利用其透支额度借钱完成期权费所需资金的支付。Fondir 公司的 透支利率是每年5.4%。 Fondir 公司的董事会想知道如何使用上述两种金融工具管理该笔未来的外币收入带来 的货币风险，以及是否有可能使用其他替代方案而不是衍生金融工具实现风险对冲。 3.短期投资 Fondir 公司将选择财务上更有利的对冲工具来对冲外币收入带来的风险。 一旦在4个 月后的5月1日收到 LL 并兑换成美元，这笔美元将在其他需要投资的地方被再投资5 个月。Fondir 公司的董事会想知道如何利用利率期货来对冲基础利率0.5%的增减变动。 Fondir公司可以以美国基准利率(目前为3.3%)减去30个基点进行短期投资。 以下是六月的期货信息： 三个月的期货合约的合约规模为$500,000。六月期货合约价格是96.10,按照100减去 年化收益率的形式报价。 在计算中，可以假设以下情况： 一 期货合约的结算在月末进行； - 根据每月时间间隔，基差在合约到期时降低为零； - 没有基差风险。 - 保证金要求可以忽略。 Fondir 公司的董事会听说，由于期货保证金的存在，期货交易可能会很昂贵。他们想 知道期货的保证金是什么,以及期货交易是否会因此而变得昂贵。
